45→import java.util.stream.Collectors;\n    46→\n    47→/**\n    48→ * An active task could only be in one of the following status:\n    49→ *\n    50→ * 1. It's assigned to one of the executors for processing.\n    51→ * 2. It's locked for committing, removal, other manipulations etc.\n    52→ * 3. Neither 1 or 2, i.e. it stays idle. This is possible if we do not have enough executors or because those tasks\n    53→ *    are not processable (e.g. because no records fetched) yet.\n    54→ */\n    55→public final class DefaultTaskManager implements TaskManager {\n    56→\n    57→    private final Time time;\n    58→    private final Logger log;\n    59→    private final TasksRegistry tasks;\n    60→    private final TopologyMetadata topologyMetadata;\n    61→\n    62→    private final Lock tasksLock = new ReentrantLock();\n    63→    private final Condition tasksCondition = tasksLock.newCondition();\n    64→    private final List<TaskId> lockedTasks = new ArrayList<>();\n    65→    private final Map<TaskId, StreamsException> uncaughtExceptions = new HashMap<>();\n    66→    private final Map<TaskId, TaskExecutor> assignedTasks = new HashMap<>();\n    67→    private final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata;\n    68→\n    69→    private final List<TaskExecutor> taskExecutors;\n    70→\n    71→    public static class DefaultTaskExecutorCreator implements TaskExecutorCreator {\n    72→        @Override\n    73→        public TaskExecutor create(final TaskManager taskManager, final String name, final Time time, final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata) {\n    74→            return new DefaultTaskExecutor(taskManager, name, time, taskExecutionMetadata);\n    75→        }\n    76→    }\n    77→\n    78→    public DefaultTaskManager(final Time time,\n    79→                              final String clientId,\n    80→                              final TasksRegistry tasks,\n    81→                              final TaskExecutorCreator executorCreator,\n    82→                              final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata,\n    83→                              final int numExecutors,\n    84→                              final TopologyMetadata topologyMetadata\n    85→                              ) {\n    86→        final String logPrefix = String.format(\"%s \", clientId);\n    87→        final LogContext logContext = new LogContext(logPrefix);\n    88→        this.log = logContext.logger(DefaultTaskManager.class);\n    89→        this.time = time;\n    90→        this.tasks = tasks;\n    91→        this.taskExecutionMetadata = taskExecutionMetadata;\n    92→        this.topologyMetadata = topologyMetadata;\n    93→        topologyMetadata.registerLifecycleObserver(new TaskTopologyObserver());\n    94→\n    95→        this.taskExecutors = new ArrayList<>(numExecutors);\n    96→        for (int i = 1; i <= numExecutors; i++) {\n    97→            final String name = clientId + \"-TaskExecutor-\" + i;\n    98→            this.taskExecutors.add(executorCreator.create(this, name, time, taskExecutionMetadata));\n    99→        }\n   100→    }\n   101→\n   102→    private class TaskTopologyObserver implements TopologyMetadata.TopologyLifecycleObserver {\n   103→        @Override\n   104→        public void onTopologyChange(final long newVersion, final Set<String> activeTopologies) {\n   105→            validateTopologyConsistency(activeTopologies);\n   106→        }\n   107→    }\n   108→\n   109→    private void validateTopologyConsistency(final Set<String> activeTopologies) {\n   110→        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   111→            for (final StreamTask task : tasks.activeInitializedTasks()) {\n   112→                final String topologyName = task.id().topologyName();\n   113→                if (topologyName != null && !activeTopologies.contains(topologyName)) {\n   114→                    log.warn(\"Task {} belongs to topology {} which is no longer active\", task.id(), topologyName);\n   115→                }\n   116→            }\n   117→        });\n   118→    }\n   119→\n   120→    @Override\n   121→    public StreamTask assignNextTask(final TaskExecutor executor) {\n   122→        return returnWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   123→            if (!taskExecutors.contains(executor)) {\n   124→                throw new IllegalArgumentException(\"The requested executor for getting next task to assign is unrecognized\");\n   125→            }\n   126→\n   127→            // the most naive scheduling algorithm for now: give the next unlocked, unassigned, and  processable task\n   128→            for (final StreamTask task : tasks.activeInitializedTasks()) {\n   129→                if (topologyMetadata.isTaskTopologyStale(task.id())) {\n   130→                    continue;\n   131→                }\n   132→                if (!assignedTasks.containsKey(task.id()) &&\n   133→                    !lockedTasks.contains(task.id()) &&\n   134→                    canProgress(task, time.milliseconds()) &&\n   135→                    !hasUncaughtException(task.id())\n   136→                ) {\n   137→\n   138→                    assignedTasks.put(task.id(), executor);\n   139→\n   140→                    log.debug(\"Assigned task {} to executor {}\", task.id(), executor.name());\n   141→\n   142→                    return task;\n   143→                }\n   144→            }\n   145→\n   146→            log.debug(\"Found no assignable task for executor {}\", executor.name());\n   147→\n   148→            return null;\n   149→        });\n   150→    }\n   151→\n   152→    @Override\n   153→    public void awaitProcessableTasks(final Supplier<Boolean> isShuttingDown) throws InterruptedException {\n   154→        final boolean interrupted = returnWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   155→            for (final StreamTask task : tasks.activeInitializedTasks()) {\n   156→                if (!assignedTasks.containsKey(task.id()) &&\n   157→                    !lockedTasks.contains(task.id()) &&\n   158→                    canProgress(task, time.milliseconds()) &&\n   159→                    !hasUncaughtException(task.id())\n   160→                ) {\n   161→                    log.debug(\"Await unblocked: returning early from await since a processable task {} was found\", task.id());\n   162→                    return false;\n   163→                }\n   164→            }\n   165→            try {\n   166→                // We re-check the shutdownRequest atomic boolean to avoid a race condition. If this thread was\n   167→                // previously interrupted while awaiting tasksCondition, it is possible to miss the signalAll that\n   168→                // is called during shutdown. If this happens, we end up blocking in the await forever.\n   169→                if (!isShuttingDown.get()) {\n   170→                    log.debug(\"Await blocking\");\n   171→                    tasksCondition.await();\n   172→                } else {\n   173→                    log.debug(\"Not awaiting since shutdown was requested\");\n   174→                }\n   175→            } catch (final InterruptedException ignored) {\n   176→                // we interrupt the thread for shut down and pause.\n   177→                // we can ignore this exception.\n   178→                log.debug(\"Await unblocked: Interrupted while waiting for processable tasks\");\n   179→                return true;\n   180→            }\n   181→            log.debug(\"Await unblocked: Woken up to check for processable tasks\");\n   182→            return false;\n   183→        });\n   184→\n   185→        if (interrupted) {\n   186→            throw new InterruptedException();\n   187→        }\n   188→    }\n   189→\n   190→    public void signalTaskExecutors() {\n   191→        log.debug(\"Waking up task executors\");\n   192→        executeWithTasksLocked(tasksCondition::signalAll);\n   193→    }\n   194→\n   195→    @Override\n   196→    public void unassignTask(final StreamTask task, final TaskExecutor executor) {\n   197→        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   198→ {\n   308→        pausedTopologies.add(topologyName);\n   309→    }\n   310→\n   311→    /**\n   312→     * Checks if a given topology is paused.\n   313→     * @param topologyName If null, assume 327→\n   328→    /**\n   329→     * Removes the topology and registers a future that listens for all threads on the older version to see the update\n   330→     */\n   331→    public KafkaFuture<Void> unregisterTopology(final KafkaFutureImpl<Void> removeTopologyFuture,\n   332→                                                final String topologyName) {\n   333→        try {\n   334→            lock();\n   335→            log.info(\"Beginning removal of NamedTopology {}, old topology version is {}\", topologyName, version.topologyVersion.get());\n   336→            version.topologyVersion.incrementAndGet();\n   337→            version.activeTopologyUpdateListeners.add(new TopologyVersionListener(topologyVersion(), removeTopologyFuture));\n   338→            final InternalTopologyBuilder removedBuilder = builders.remove(topologyName);\n   339→            removedBuilder.fullSourceTopicNames().forEach(allInputTopics::remove);\n   340→            removedBuilder.allSourcePatternStrings().forEach(allInputTopics::remove);\n   341→            notifyLifecycleObservers();\n   342→            log.info(\"Finished removing NamedTopology {}, topology version was updated to {}\", topologyName, version.topologyVersion.get());\n   343→        } catch (final Throwable throwable) {\n   344→            log.error(\"Failed to remove NamedTopology {}, please retry.\", topologyName);\n   345→            removeTopologyFuture.completeExceptionally(throwable);\n   346→        } finally {\n   347→            unlock();\n   348→        }\n   349→        return removeTopologyFuture;\n   350→    }\n   351→\n   352→    public TaskConfig taskConfig(final TaskId taskId) {\n   353→        final InternalTopologyBuilder builder = lookupBuilderForTask(taskId);\n   354→        return builder.topologyConfigs().getTaskConfig();\n   355→    }\n   356→\n   357→    public void buildAndRewriteTopology() {\n   358→        applyToEachBuilder(this::buildAndVerifyTopology);\n   359→    }\n   360→\n   361→    private void buildAndVerifyTopology(final InternalTopologyBuilder builder) {\n   362→        builder.rewriteTopology(config);\n   363→        builder.buildTopology();\n   364→\n   365→        final Set<String> allInputTopicsCopy = new HashSet<>(allInputTopics);\n   366→\n   367→        // As we go, check each topology for overlap in the set of input topics/patterns\n   368→        final int numInputTopics = allInputTopicsCopy.size();\n   369→        final List<String> inputTopics = builder.fullSourceTopicNames();\n   370→        final Collection<String> inputPatterns = builder.allSourcePatternStrings();\n   371→\n   372→        final Set<String> newInputTopics = new HashSet<>(inputTopics);\n   373→        newInputTopics.addAll(inputPatterns);\n   374→\n   375→        final int numNewInputTopics = newInputTopics.size();\n   376→        allInputTopicsCopy.addAll(newInputTopics);\n   377→\n   378→        if (allInputTopicsCopy.size() != numInputTopics + numNewInputTopics) {\n   379→            inputTopics.retainAll(allInputTopicsCopy);\n   380→            inputPatterns.retainAll(allInputTopicsCopy);\n   381→            log.error(\"Tried to add the NamedTopology {} but it had overlap with other input topics {} or patterns {}\",\n   382→                      builder.topologyName(), inputTopics, inputPatterns);\n   383→            throw new TopologyException(\"Named Topologies may not subscribe to the same input topics or patterns\");\n   384→        }\n   385→\n   386→        final ProcessorTopology globalTopology = builder.buildGlobalStateTopology();\n   387→        if (globalTopology != null) {\n   388→            if (builder.topologyName() != null) {\n   389→                throw new IllegalStateException(\"Global state stores are not supported with Named Topologies\");\n   390→            } else if (this.globalTopology != null) {\n   391→                throw new TopologyException(\"Topology builder had global state, but global topology has already been set\");\n   392→            } else {\n   393→                this.globalTopology = globalTopology;\n   394→                globalStateStores.putAll(builder.globalStateStores());\n   395→            }\n   396→        }\n   397→        allInputTopics.addAll(newInputTopics);\n   398→    }\n   399→\n   400→    public int numStreamThreads(final StreamsConfig config) {\n   401→        final int configuredNumStreamThreads = config.getInt(StreamsConfig.NUM_STREAM_THREADS_CONFIG);\n   402→\n   403→        // If there are named topologies but some are empty, this indicates a bug in user code\n   404→        if (hasNamedTopologies()) {\n   405→            if (hasNoLocalTopology()) {\n   406→                log.error(\"Detected a named topology with no input topics, a named topology may not be empty.\");\n   407→                throw new TopologyException(\"Topology has no stream threads and no global threads, \" +\n   408→                                                \"must subscribe to at least one source topic or pattern.\");\n   409→            }\n   410→        } else {\n   411→            // If both the global and non-global topologies are empty, this indicates a bug in user code\n   412→            if (hasNoLocalTopology() && !hasGlobalTopology()) {\n   413→                log.error(\"Topology with no input topics will create no stream threads and no global thread.\");\n   414→                throw new TopologyException(\"Topology has no stream threads and no global threads, \" +\n   415→                                                \"must subscribe to at least one source topic or global table.\");\n   416→            }\n   417→        }\n   418→\n   419→        // Lastly we check for an empty non-global topology and override the threads to zero if set otherwise\n   420→        if (configuredNumStreamThreads != 0 && hasNoLocalTopology()) {\n   421→            log.info(\"Overriding number of StreamThreads to zero for global-only topology\");\n   422→            return 0;\n   423→        }\n   424→\n   425→        return configuredNumStreamThreads;\n   426→    }\n   427→\n   428→    /**\n   429→     * @return true iff the app is using named one or more input topics and\n   122→ * sends output to zero, one, or more output topics.\n   123→ * <p>\n   124→ * The computational logic can be specified either by using the {@link Topology} to define a DAG topology of\n   125→ * {@link org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.api.Processor}s or by using the {@link StreamsBuilder} which provides the high-level DSL to define\n   126→ * transformations.\n   127→ * <p>\n   128→ * One {@code KafkaStreams} instance can contain one or more threads specified in the configs for the processing work.\n   129→ * <p>\n   130→ * A {@code KafkaStreams} instance can co-ordinate with any other instances with the same\n   131→ * {@link StreamsConfig#APPLICATION_ID_CONFIG application ID} (whether in the same process, on other processes on this\n   132→ * machine, or on remote machines) as a single (possibly distributed) stream processing application.\n   133→ * These instances will divide up the work based on the assignment of the input topic partitions so that all partitions\n   134→ * are being consumed.\n   135→ * If instances are added or fail, all (remaining) instances will rebalance the partition assignment among themselves\n   136→ * to balance processing load and ensure that all input topic partitions are processed.\n   137→ * <p>\n   138→ * Internally a {@code KafkaStreams} instance contains a normal {@link KafkaProducer} and {@link KafkaConsumer} instance\n   139→ * that is used for reading input and writing output.\n   140→ * <p>\n   141→ * A simple example might look like this:\n   142→ * <pre>{@code\n   143→ * Properties props = new Properties();\n   144→ * props.put(StreamsConfig.APPLICATION_ID_CONFIG, \"my-stream-processing-application\");\n   145→ * props.put(StreamsConfig.DEFAULT_VALUE_SERDE_CLASS_CONFIG, Serdes.String().getClass());\n   148→ *\n   149→ * StreamsBuilder builder = new StreamsBuilder();\n   150→ * builder.<String, String>stream(\"my-input-topic\").mapValues(value -> StreamThread extends Thread implements ProcessingThread {\n   111→\n   112→    private static final String THREAD_ID_SUBSTRING = \"-StreamThread-\";\n   113→    private static final String STATE_UPDATER_ID_SUBSTRING = \"-StateUpdater-\";\n   114→\n   115→    /**\n   116→     * Stream thread states are the possible states that a stream thread can be in.\n   117→     * A thread must only be in one state at a time\n   118→     * The expected state transitions with the following defined states is:\n   119→     *\n   120→     * <pre>\n   121→     *                 +-------------+\n   122→     *          +<---- | Created (0) |\n   123→     *          |      +-----+-------+\n   124→     *          |            |\n   125→     *          |            v\n   126→     *          |      +-----+-------+\n   127→     *          +<---- | Starting (1)|----->+\n   128→     *          |      +-----+-------+      |\n   129→     *          |                           |\n   130→     *          |            +<----------+  |\n   131→     *          |            |           |  |\n   132→     *          |            v           |  |\n   133→     *          |      +-----+-------+   |  |\n   134→     *          +<---- | Partitions  | --+  |\n   135→     *          |      | Revoked (2) | <----+\n   136→     *          |      +-----+-------+      |\n   137→     *          |           |  ^            |\n   138→     *          |           v  |            |\n   139→     *          |      +-----+-------+      |\n   140→     *          +<---- | Partitions  |      |\n   141→     *          |      | Assigned (3)| <----+\n   142→     *          |      +-----+-------+      |\n   143→     *          |            |              |\n   144→     *          |            +<----------+  |\n   145→     *          |            |           |  |\n   146→     *          |            v           |  |\n   147→     *          |      +-----+-------+   |  |\n   148→     *          |      |             | --+  |\n   149→     *          |      | Running (4) | ---->+\n   150→     *          |      +-----+-------+\n   151→     *          |            |\n   152→     *          |            v\n   153→     *          |      +-----+-------+\n   154→     *          +----> | Pending     |\n   155→     *                 | Shutdown (5)|\n   156→     *                 +-----+-------+\n   157→     *                       |\n   158→     *                       v\n   159→     *                 +-----+-------+\n   160→     *                 | Dead (6)    |\n   161→     *                 +-------------+\n   162→     * </pre>\n   163→     *\n   164→     * Note the following:\n   165→     * <ul>\n   166→     *     <li>Any state can go to PENDING_SHUTDOWN. That is because streams can be closed at any time.</li>\n   167→     *     <li>\n   168→     *         State PENDING_SHUTDOWN may want to transit to some other states other than DEAD,\n   169→     *         in the corner case when the shutdown is triggered while the thread is still in the rebalance loop.\n   170→     *         In this case we will forbid the transition but will not treat as an error.\n   171→     *     </li>\n   172→     *     <li>\n   173→     *         State PARTITIONS_REVOKED may want transit to itself indefinitely, in the corner case when\n   174→     *         the coordinator repeatedly fails in-between revoking partitions and assigning new partitions.\n   175→     *         Also during streams instance start up PARTITIONS_REVOKED may want to transit to itself as well.\n   176→     *         In this case we will allow the transition but it will be a no-op as the set of revoked partitions\n   177→     *         should be empty.\n   178→     *     </li>\n   179→     * </ul>\n   180→     */\n   181→    public enum State implements ThreadStateTransitionValidator {\n   182→\n   183→        CREATED(1, 5),                    // 0\n   184→        STARTING(2, 3, 5),                // 1\n   185→        PARTITIONS_REVOKED(2, 3, 5),      // 2\n   186→        PARTITIONS_ASSIGNED(2, 3, 4, 5),  // 3\n   187→        RUNNING(2, 3, 4, 5),              // 4\n   188→        PENDING_SHUTDOWN(6),              // 5\n   189→        DEAD;                             // 6\n   190→\n   191→        private final Set<Integer> validTransitions = new HashSet<>();\n   192→\n   193→        State(final Integer... validTransitions) {\n   194→            this.validTransitions.addAll(Arrays.asList(validTransitions));\n   195→        }\n   196→\n   197→        public boolean isAlive() {\n   198→            return equals(RUNNING) || equals(STARTING) || equals(PARTITIONS_REVOKED) || equals(PARTITIONS_ASSIGNED);\n   199→        }\n   200→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01MaeeDYJJ2DK8h3FvAnVj6t","session_id":"bea6d363-fa8a-4106-81c5-e4c086a94109","uuid":"588a99d2-e3b1-40f0-97e6-3f3ee9f73169"}

31→import org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.Cancellable;\n    32→import org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.PunctuationType;\n    33→import org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.Punctuator;\n    34→import org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.StateStore;\n    35→import org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.TaskId;\n    36→import org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.To;\n    37→import com.fasterxml.jackson.annotation.JsonIgnoreProperties;\n    45→import com.fasterxml.jackson.annotation.JsonProperty;\n    46→import com.fasterxml.jackson.databind.ObjectMapper;\n    47→\n    48→import org.slf4j.Logger;\n    49→import org.slf4j.LoggerFactory;\n    50→\n    51→import java.io.File;\n    52→import java.io.FileFilter;\n    53→import java.io.IOException;\n    54→import java.nio.channels.FileChannel;\n    55→import java.nio.channels.FileLock;\n    56→import java.nio.channels.OverlappingFileLockException;\n    57→import java.nio.file.Files;\n    58→import java.nio.file.Path;\n    59→import java.nio.file.StandardOpenOption;\n    60→import java.nio.file.attribute.PosixFilePermission;\n    61→import java.time.Duration;\n    62→import java.time.Instant;\n    63→import java.util.ArrayList;\n    64→import java.util.Arrays;\n    65→import java.util.Collection;\n    66→import java.util.Collections;\n    67→import java.util.EnumSet;\n    68→import java.util.List;\n    69→import java.util.Map;\n    70→import java.util.Objects;\n    71→import java.util.Set;\n    72→import java.util.UUID;\n    73→import java.util.concurrent.ConcurrentHashMap;\n    74→import java.util.concurrent.ConcurrentMap;\n    75→import java.util.concurrent.ConcurrentSkipListSet;\n    76→import java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicReference;\n    77→import java.util.regex.Pattern;\n    78→import java.util.stream.Collectors;\n    79→\n    80→import static org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.internals.StateManagerUtil.CHECKPOINT_FILE_NAME;\n    81→import static org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.internals.StateManagerUtil.parseTaskDirectoryName;\n    82→\n    83→/**\n    84→ * Manages the directories where the state of Tasks owned by a {@link StreamThread} are\n    85→ * stored. Handles creation/locking/unlocking/cleaning of the Task Directories. This class is not\n    86→ * thread-safe.\n    87→ */\n    88→public class StateDirectory implements AutoCloseable {\n    89→\n    90→    private static final Pattern TASK_DIR_PATH_NAME = Pattern.compile(\"\\\\d+_\\\\d+\");\n    91→    private static final Pattern NAMED_TOPOLOGY_DIR_PATH_NAME = Pattern.compile(\"__.+__\"); // named topology dirs follow '__Topology-Name__'\n    92→    private static final Logger log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(StateDirectory.class);\n    93→    static final String LOCK_FILE_NAME = \".lock\";\n    94→\n    95→    /* The process file is used to persist the process id across restarts.\n    96→     * For compatibility reasons you should only ever add fields to the json schema\n    97→     */\n    98→    static final String PROCESS_FILE_NAME = \"kafka-streams-process-metadata\";\n    99→\n   100→    @JsonIgnoreProperties(ignoreUnknown = true)\n   101→    static class StateDirectoryProcessFile {\n   102→        @JsonProperty\n   103→        private final UUID processId;\n   104→\n   105→        // required by jackson -- do not remove, your IDE may be warning that this is unused but it's lying to you\n   106→        public StateDirectoryProcessFile() {\n   107→            this.processId = null;\n   108→        }\n   109→\n   110→        StateDirectoryProcessFile(final UUID processId) {\n   111→            this.processId = processId;\n   112→        }\n   113→    }\n   114→\n   115→    private final Object taskDirCreationLock = new Object();\n   116→    private final Time time;\n   117→    private final String appId;\n   118→    private final File stateDir;\n   119→    private final boolean hasPersistentStores;\n   120→    private final boolean hasNamedTopologies;\n   121→\n   122→    private final ConcurrentMap<TaskId, Thread> lockedTasksToOwner = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();\n   123→\n   124→    private FileChannel stateDirLockChannel;\n   125→    private FileLock stateDirLock;\n   126→\n   127→    private final StreamsConfig config;\n   128→    private final Set<TaskId> tasksInLocalState = new ConcurrentSkipListSet<>();\n   129→    private final Map<TaskId, Long> taskOffsetSums = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();\n   130→\n   131→    /**\n   132→     * Ensures that the state base directory as well as the application's sub-directory are created.\n   133→     *\n   134→     * @param config              streams application configuration to read the root state directory path\n   135→     * @param time                system timer used to execute periodic cleanup procedure\n   136→     * @param hasPersistentStores only when the application's topology does have stores persisted on local file\n   137→     *                            system, we would go ahead and auto-create the corresponding application / task / store\n   138→     *                            directories whenever necessary; otherwise no directories would be created.\n   139→     * @param hasNamedTopologies  whether this application is composed of independent named topologies\n   140→     *\n   141→     * @throws ProcessorStateException if the base state directory or application state directory does not exist\n   142→     *                                 and could not be created when hasPersistentStores is enabled.\n   143→     */\n   144→    public StateDirectory(final StreamsConfig config, final Time time, final boolean hasPersistentStores, final boolean hasNamedTopologies) {\n   145→        this.time = time;\n   146→        this.hasPersistentStores = hasPersistentStores;\n   147→        this.hasNamedTopologies = hasNamedTopologies;\n   148→        this.appId = config.getString(StreamsConfig.APPLICATION_ID_CONFIG);\n   149→        this.config = config;\n   150→        final String stateDirName = config.getString(StreamsConfig.STATE_DIR_CONFIG);\n   151→        final File baseDir = new File(stateDirName);\n   152→        stateDir = new File(baseDir, appId);\n   153→\n   154→        if (this.hasPersistentStores) {\n   155→            if (!baseDir.exists() && !baseDir.mkdirs()) {\n   156→                throw new ProcessorStateException(\n   157→                    String.format(\"base state directory [%s] doesn't exist and couldn't be created\", stateDirName));\n   158→            }\n   159→            if (!stateDir.exists() && !stateDir.mkdir()) {\n   160→                throw new ProcessorStateException(\n   161→                    String.format(\"state directory [%s] doesn't exist and couldn't be created\", stateDir.getPath()));\n   162→            } else if (stateDir.exists() && !stateDir.isDirectory()) {\n   163→                throw new ProcessorStateException(\n   164→                    String.format(\"state directory [%s] can't be created as there is an existing file with the same name\", stateDir.getPath()));\n   165→            }\n   166→\n   167→            if (stateDirName.startsWith(System.getProperty(\"java.io.tmpdir\"))) {\n   168→                log.warn(\"Using an OS temp directory in the state.dir property can cause failures with writing\" +\n   169→                    \" the checkpoint file due to the fact that this directory can be cleared by the OS.\" +\n   170→                    \" Resolved state.dir: [\" + stateDirName + \"]\");\n   171→\n   172→            }\n   173→            // change the dir permission to \"rwxr-x---\" to avoid world readable\n   174→            configurePermissions(baseDir);\n   175→            configurePermissions(stateDir);\n   176→        }\n   177→    }\n   178→\n   179→    private void configurePermissions(final File file) {\n   180→        final Path path = file.toPath();\n   181→        if (path.getFileSystem().supportedFileAttributeViews().contains(\"posix\")) {\n   182→            final Set<PosixFilePermission> perms = EnumSet.of(\n   183→                    PosixFilePermission.OWNER_READ,\n   184→                    PosixFilePermission.OWNER_WRITE,\n   185→                    PosixFilePermission.OWNER_EXECUTE,\n   186→                    PosixFilePermission.GROUP_READ,\n   187→                    PosixFilePermission.GROUP_EXECUTE\n   188→            );\n   189→            if (config.getBoolean(StreamsConfig.ALLOW_OS_GROUP_WRITE_ACCESS_CONFIG)) {\n   190→                perms.add(PosixFilePermission.GROUP_WRITE);\n   191→            }\n   192→            try {\n   193→                Files.setPosixFilePermissions(path, perms);\n   194→            } catch (final IOException e) {\n   195→                log.error(\"Error changing permissions for the directory {} \", path, e);\n   196→            }\n   197→        } else {\n   198→            boolean set = file.setReadable(true, true);\n   199→            set &= file.setWritable(true, true);\n   200→            set &= file.setExecutable(true, true);\n   201→            if (!set) {\n   202→                log.error(\"Failed to change permissions for the directory {}\", file);\n   203→            }\n   204→        }\n   205→    }\n   206→\n   207→    /**\n   208→     * @return true if the state directory was successfully locked\n   209→     */\n   210→    private boolean lockStateDirectory() {\n   211→        final File lockFile = new File(stateDir, LOCK_FILE_NAME);\n   212→        try {\n   213→            stateDirLockChannel = FileChannel.open(lockFile.toPath(), StandardOpenOption.CREATE, StandardOpenOption.WRITE);\n   214→            stateDirLock = tryLock(stateDirLockChannel);\n   215→        } catch (final IOException e) {\n   216→            log.error(\"Unable to lock the state directory due to unexpected exception\", e);\n   217→            throw new ProcessorStateException(String.format(\"Failed to lock the state directory [%s] during startup\",\n   218→                stateDir.getAbsolutePath()), e);\n   219→        }\n   220→        return stateDirLock != null;\n   221→    }\n   222→\n   223→    /**\n   224→     * @throws LockException If another process already locks any of StateDirectory\n   225→     * @throws ProcessorStateException if any of task directory does not exist and could not be created\n   226→    */\n   227→    public void initializeStartupStores(final TopologyMetadata topologyMetadata,\n   228→                                        final LogContext logContext,\n   229→                                        final StreamsMetricsImpl metricsImpl) {\n   230→        final List<TaskDirectory> nonEmptyTaskDirectories = listNonEmptyTaskDirectories();\n   231→        if (hasPersistentStores && !nonEmptyTaskDirectories.isEmpty()) {\n   232→            final boolean eosEnabled = StreamsConfigUtils.eosEnabled(config);\n   233→\n   234→            // Initialize thread-specific resources needed to open stores in the state directory\n   235→            final String threadLogPrefix = String.format(\"[%s]\", Thread.currentThread().getName());\n   236→            final ThreadCache dummyCache = new ThreadCache(new LogContext(threadLogPrefix), 0L, metricsImpl);\n   237→\n   238→            // discover all non-empty task directories in StateDirectory\n   239→            for (final TaskDirectory taskDirectory : nonEmptyTaskDirectories) {\n   240→                final String dirName = taskDirectory.file().getName();\n   241→                final TaskId task = parseTaskDirectoryName(dirName, taskDirectory.namedTopology());\n   242→                final ProcessorTopology subTopology = topologyMetadata.buildSubtopology(task);\n   243→\n   244→                // we still check if the task's sub-topology is stateful, even though we know its directory contains state,\n   245→                // because it's possible that the topology has changed since that data was written, and is now stateless\n   246→                // this therefore prevents us from creating unnecessary stores just because of some left-over state\n   247→                if (subTopology.hasStateWithChangelogs()) {\n   248→                    final Set<TopicPartition> inputPartitions = topologyMetadata.nodeToSourceTopics(task).values().stream()\n   249→                            .flatMap(Collection::stream)\n   250→                            .map(t -> new TopicPartition(t, task.partition()))\n   251→                            .collect(Collectors.toSet());\n   252→                    // Open a temporary state manager that will open the stores inside the subtopology\n   253→                    final ProcessorStateManager temporaryStateManager = ProcessorStateManager.createStartupTaskStateManager(\n   254→                        task,\n   255→                        eosEnabled,\n   256→                        logContext,\n   257→                        this,\n   258→                        subTopology.storeToChangelogTopic(),\n   259→                        inputPartitions\n   260→                    );\n   261→\n   262→                    final StartupContext initContext = new StartupContext(task, config, temporaryStateManager, metricsImpl, dummyCache);\n   263→                    try {\n   264→                        // We only handle TaskCorruptedException at this point. Any other exception is considered fatal.\n   265→                        StateManagerUtil.registerStateStores(log, threadLogPrefix, subTopology, temporaryStateManager, this, initContext);\n   266→                        temporaryStateManager.checkpoint();\n   267→                    } catch (final TaskCorruptedException tce) {\n   268→                        // At this point, we only log a warning and continue with the startup store initialization.\n   269→                        // The task-corrupted exception will be handled in the first Task assignment phase.\n   270→                        log.warn(\"Failed to register startup state stores for task {}: {}\", task, tce.getMessage());\n   271→                    } finally {\n   272→                        // Make sure the state manager writes the local checkpoint file before closing the stores\n   273→                        // This will be replaced in the future when removing the checkpoint file dependency.\n   274→                        temporaryStateManager.close();\n   275→                    }\n   276→                    tasksInLocalState.add(task);\n   277→                }\n   278→            }\n   279→        }\n   280→    }\n   281→\n   282→    public boolean hasStartupTasks() {\n   283→        return !tasksInLocalState.isEmpty();\n   284→    }\n   285→\n   286→    public synchronized boolean removeStartupState(final TaskId taskId) {\n   287→        final boolean removed = tasksInLocalState.remove(taskId);\n   288→        if (removed) {\n   289→            lockedTasksToOwner.put(taskId, Thread.currentThread());\n   290→        }\n   291→        return removed;\n   292→    }\n   293→\n   294→\n   295→    private void unlockStartupStores() {\n   296→        for (final TaskId task : tasksInLocalState) {\n   297→            unlock(task);\n   298→        }\n   299→    }\n   300→\n   301→    public Map<TaskId, Long> taskOffsetSums(final Set<TaskId> tasks) {\n   302→        return taskOffsetSums.entrySet().stream()\n   303→                .filter(e -> tasks.contains(e.getKey()))\n   304→                .collect(Collectors.toMap(Map.Entry::getKey, Map.Entry::getValue));\n   305→    }\n   306→\n   307→    public void updateTaskOffsets(final TaskId taskId, final Map<TopicPartition, Long> changelogOffsets) {\n   308→        if (!changelogOffsets.isEmpty()) {\n   309→            taskOffsetSums.put(taskId, sumOfChangelogOffsets(taskId, changelogOffsets));\n   310→        }\n   311→    }\n   312→\n   313→    public void removeTaskOffsets(final TaskId taskId) {\n   314→        taskOffsetSums.remove(taskId);\n   315→    }\n   316→\n   317→    private long sumOfChangelogOffsets(final TaskId taskId, final Map<TopicPartition, Long> changelogOffsets) {\n   318→        long offsetSum = 0L;\n   319→        for (final Map.Entry<TopicPartition, Long> changelogEntry : changelogOffsets.entrySet()) {\n   320→            final long offset = changelogEntry.getValue();\n   321→\n   322→            if (offset != OffsetCheckpoint.OFFSET_UNKNOWN) {\n   323→                if (offset < 0) {\n   324→                    throw new StreamsException(\n   325→                            new IllegalStateException(\"Expected not to get a sentinel offset, but got: \" + changelogEntry),\n   326→                            taskId);\n   327→                }\n   328→                offsetSum += offset;\n   329→                if (offsetSum < 0) {\n   330→                    log.warn(\"Sum of changelog offsets for task {} overflowed, pinning to Long.MAX_VALUE\", taskId);\n   331→                    return Long.MAX_VALUE;\n   332→                }\n   333→            }\n   334→        }\n   335→\n   336→        return offsetSum;\n   337→    }\n   338→\n   339→    public UUID initializeProcessId() {\n   340→        if (!hasPersistentStores) {\n   341→            final UUID processId = UUID.randomUUID();\n   342→            log.info(\"Created new process id: {}\", processId);\n   343→            return processId;\n   344→        }\n   345→\n   346→        if (!lockStateDirectory()) {\n   347→            log.error(\"Unable to obtain lock as state directory is already locked by another process\");\n   348→            throw new StreamsException(String.format(\"Unable to initialize state, this can happen if multiple instances of \" +\n   349→                                           \"Kafka Streams are running in the same state directory \" +\n   350→                                           \"(current state directory is [%s]\", stateDir.getAbsolutePath()));\n   351→        }\n   352→\n   353→        final File processFile = new File(stateDir, PROCESS_FILE_NAME);\n   354→        final ObjectMapper mapper = new ObjectMapper();\n   355→\n   356→        try {\n   357→            if (processFile.exists()) {\n   358→                try {\n   359→                    final StateDirectoryProcessFile processFileData = mapper.readValue(processFile, StateDirectoryProcessFile.class);\n   360→                    log.info(\"Reading UUID from process file: {}\", processFileData.processId);\n   361→                    if (processFileData.processId != null) {\n   362→                        return processFileData.processId;\n   363→                    }\n   364→                } catch (final Exception e) {\n   365→                    log.warn(\"Failed to read json process file\", e);\n   366→                }\n   367→            }\n   368→\n   369→            final StateDirectoryProcessFile processFileData = new StateDirectoryProcessFile(UUID.randomUUID());\n   370→            log.info(\"No process id found on disk, got fresh process id {}\", processFileData.processId);\n   371→\n   372→            mapper.writeValue(processFile, processFileData);\n   373→            return processFileData.processId;\n   374→        } catch (final IOException e) {\n   375→            log.error(\"Unable to read/write process file due to unexpected exception\", e);\n   376→            throw new ProcessorStateException(e);\n   377→        }\n   378→    }\n   379→\n   380→    /**\n   381→     * Get or create the directory for the provided {@link TaskId}.\n   382→     * @return directory for the {@link TaskId}\n   383→     * @throws ProcessorStateException if the task directory does not exist and could not be created\n   384→     */\n   385→    public File getOrCreateDirectoryForTask(final TaskId taskId) {\n   386→        final File taskParentDir = getTaskDirectoryParentName(taskId);\n   387→        final File taskDir = new File(taskParentDir, StateManagerUtil.toTaskDirString(taskId));\n   388→        if (hasPersistentStores) {\n   389→            if (!taskDir.exists()) {\n   390→                synchronized (taskDirCreationLock) {\n   391→                    // to avoid a race condition, we need to check again if the directory does not exist:\n   392→                    // otherwise, two threads might pass the outer `if` (and enter the `then` block),\n   393→                    // one blocks on `synchronized` while the other creates the directory,\n   394→                    // and the blocking one fails when trying to create it after it's unblocked\n   395→                    if (!taskParentDir.exists() && !taskParentDir.mkdir()) {\n   396→                        throw new ProcessorStateException(\n   397→                            String.format(\"Parent [%s] of task directory [%s] doesn't exist and couldn't be created\",\n   398→                                taskParentDir.getPath(), taskDir.getPath()));\n   399→                    }\n   400→                    if (!taskDir.exists() && !taskDir.mkdir()) {\n   401→                        throw new ProcessorStateException(\n   402→                            String.format(\"task directory [%s] doesn't exist and couldn't be created\", taskDir.getPath()));\n   403→                    }\n   404→                }\n   405→            } else if (!taskDir.isDirectory()) {\n   406→                throw new ProcessorStateException(\n   407→                    String.format(\"state directory [%s] can't be created as there is an existing file with the same name\", taskDir.getPath()));\n   408→            }\n   409→        }\n   410→        return taskDir;\n   411→    }\n   412→\n   413→    private File getTaskDirectoryParentName(final TaskId taskId) {\n   414→        final String namedTopology = taskId.topologyName();\n   415→        if (namedTopology != null) {\n   416→            if (!hasNamedTopologies) {\n   417→                throw new IllegalStateException(\"Tried to lookup taskId with named topology, but StateDirectory thinks hasNamedTopologies = false\");\n   418→            }\n   419→            return new File(stateDir, getNamedTopologyDirName(namedTopology));\n   420→        } else {\n   421→            return stateDir;\n   422→        }\n   423→    }\n   424→\n   425→    private String getNamedTopologyDirName(final String topologyName) {\n   426→        return \"__\" + topologyName + \"__\";\n   427→    }\n   428→\n   429→    /**\n   430→     * @return The File handle for the checkpoint in the given task's directory\n   431→     */\n   432→    File checkpointFileFor(final TaskId taskId) {\n   433→        return new File(getOrCreateDirectoryForTask(taskId), StateManagerUtil.CHECKPOINT_FILE_NAME);\n   434→    }\n   435→\n   436→    /**\n   437→     * Decide if the directory of the task is empty or not\n   438→     */\n   439→    boolean directoryForTaskIsEmpty(final TaskId taskId) {\n   440→        final File taskDir = getOrCreateDirectoryForTask(taskId);\n   441→\n   442→        return taskDirIsEmpty(taskDir);\n   443→    }\n   444→\n   445→    private boolean taskDirIsEmpty(final File taskDir) {\n   446→        final File[] storeDirs = taskDir.listFiles(pathname ->\n   447→                !pathname.getName().equals(CHECKPOINT_FILE_NAME));\n   448→\n   449→        boolean taskDirEmpty = true;\n   450→\n   451→        // if the task is stateless, storeDirs would be null\n   452→        if (storeDirs != null && storeDirs.length > 0) {\n   453→            for (final File file : storeDirs) {\n   454→                // We removed the task directory locking but some upgrading applications may still have old lock files on disk,\n   455→                // we just lazily delete those in this method since it's the only thing that would be affected by these\n   456→                if (file.getName().endsWith(LOCK_FILE_NAME)) {\n   457→                    if (!file.delete()) {\n   458→                        // If we hit an error deleting this just ignore it and move on, we'll retry again at some point\n   459→                        log.warn(\"Error encountered deleting lock file in {}\", taskDir);\n   460→                    }\n   461→                } else {\n   462→                    // If it's not a lock file then the directory is not empty,\n   463→                    // but finish up the loop in case there's a lock file left to delete\n   464→                    log.trace(\"TaskDir {} was not empty, found {}\", taskDir, file);\n   465→                    taskDirEmpty = false;\n   466→                }\n   467→            }\n   468→        }\n   469→        return taskDirEmpty;\n   470→    }\n   471→\n   472→    /**\n   473→     * Get or create the directory for the global stores.\n   474→     * @return directory for the global stores\n   475→     * @throws ProcessorStateException if the global store directory does not exists and could not be created\n   476→     */\n   477→    File globalStateDir() {\n   478→        final File dir = new File(stateDir, \"global\");\n   479→        if (hasPersistentStores) {\n   480→            if (!dir.exists() && !dir.mkdir()) {\n   481→                throw new ProcessorStateException(\n   482→                    String.format(\"global state directory [%s] doesn't exist and couldn't be created\", dir.getPath()));\n   483→            } else if (dir.exists() && !dir.isDirectory()) {\n   484→                throw new ProcessorStateException(\n   485→                    String.format(\"global state directory [%s] can't be created as there is an existing file with the same name\", dir.getPath()));\n   486→            }\n   487→        }\n   488→        return dir;\n   489→    }\n   490→\n   491→    private String logPrefix() {\n   492→        return String.format(\"stream-thread [%s]\", Thread.currentThread().getName());\n   493→    }\n   494→\n   495→    /**\n   496→     * Get the lock for the {@link TaskId}s directory if it is available\n   497→     * @param taskId task id\n   498→     * @return true if successful\n   499→     */\n   500→    synchronized boolean lock(final TaskId taskId) {\n   501→        if (!hasPersistentStores) {\n   502→            return true;\n   503→        }\n   504→\n   505→        final Thread lockOwner = lockedTasksToOwner.get(taskId);\n   506→        if (lockOwner != null) {\n   507→            if (lockOwner.equals(Thread.currentThread())) {\n   508→                log.trace(\"{} Found cached state dir lock for task {}\", logPrefix(), taskId);\n   509→                // we already own the lock\n   510→                return true;\n   511→            } else {\n   512→                // another thread owns the lock\n   513→                return false;\n   514→            }\n   515→        } else if (!stateDir.exists()) {\n   516→            log.error(\"Tried to lock task directory for {} but the state directory does not exist\", taskId);\n   517→            throw new IllegalStateException(\"The state directory has been deleted\");\n   518→        } else {\n   519→            lockedTasksToOwner.put(taskId, Thread.currentThread());\n   520→            return true;\n   521→        }\n   522→    }\n   523→\n   524→    /**\n   525→     * Unlock the state directory for the given {@link TaskId}.\n   526→     */\n   527→    synchronized void unlock(final TaskId taskId) {\n   528→        final Thread lockOwner = lockedTasksToOwner.get(taskId);\n   529→        if (lockOwner != null && lockOwner.equals(Thread.currentThread())) {\n   530→            lockedTasksToOwner.remove(taskId);\n   531→            log.debug(\"{} Released state dir lock for task {}\", logPrefix(), taskId);\n   532→        }\n   533→    }\n   534→\n   535→    /**\n   536→     * Expose for tests.\n   537→     */\n   538→    Thread lockOwner(final TaskId taskId) {\n   539→        return lockedTasksToOwner.get(taskId);\n   540→    }\n   541→\n   542→    @Override\n   543→    public void close() {\n   544→        if (hasPersistentStores) {\n   545→            unlockStartupStores();\n   546→            taskOffsetSums.clear();\n   547→            try {\n   548→                stateDirLock.release();\n   549→                stateDirLockChannel.close();\n   550→\n   551→                stateDirLock = null;\n   552→                stateDirLockChannel = null;\n   553→            } catch (final IOException e) {\n   554→                log.error(\"Unexpected exception while unlocking the state dir\", e);\n   555→                throw new StreamsException(String.format(\"Failed to release the lock on the state directory [%s]\", stateDir.getAbsolutePath()), e);\n   556→            }\n   557→\n   558→            // all threads should be stopped and cleaned up by now, so none should remain holding a lock\n   559→            if (!lockedTasksToOwner.isEmpty()) {\n   560→                log.error(\"Some task directories still locked while closing state, this indicates unclean shutdown: {}\", lockedTasksToOwner);\n   561→            }\n   562→        }\n   563→    }\n   564→\n   565→    public synchronized void clean() {\n   566→        try {\n   567→            cleanStateAndTaskDirectoriesCalledByUser();\n   568→        } catch (final Exception e) {\n   569→            throw new StreamsException(e);\n   570→        }\n   571→\n   572→        try {\n   573→            if (stateDir.exists()) {\n   574→                Utils.delete(globalStateDir().getAbsoluteFile());\n   575→            }\n   576→        } catch (final IOException exception) {\n   577→            log.error(\n   578→                String.format(\"%s Failed to delete global state directory of %s due to an unexpected exception\",\n   579→                    logPrefix(), appId),\n   580→                exception\n   581→            );\n   582→            throw new StreamsException(exception);\n   583→        }\n   584→\n   585→        try {\n   586→            if (hasPersistentStores && stateDir.exists() && !stateDir.delete()) {\n   587→                log.warn(\n   588→                    String.format(\"%s Failed to delete state store directory of %s for it is not empty\",\n   589→                        logPrefix(), stateDir.getAbsolutePath())\n   590→                );\n   591→            }\n   592→        } catch (final SecurityException exception) {\n   593→            log.error(\n   594→                String.format(\"%s Failed to delete state store directory of %s due to an unexpected exception\",\n   595→                    logPrefix(), stateDir.getAbsolutePath()),\n   596→                exception\n   597→            );\n   598→            throw new StreamsException(exception);\n   599→        }\n   600→    }\n   601→\n   602→    /**\n   603→     * Remove the directories for any {@link TaskId}s that are no-longer\n   604→     * owned by this {@link StreamThread} and aren't locked by either\n   605→     * another process or another {@link StreamThread}\n   606→     * @param cleanupDelayMs only remove directories if they haven't been modified for at least\n   607→     *                       this amount of time (milliseconds)\n   608→     */\n   609→    public synchronized void cleanRemovedTasks(final long cleanupDelayMs) {\n   610→        try {\n   611→            cleanRemovedTasksCalledByCleanerThread(cleanupDelayMs);\n   612→        } catch (final Exception cannotHappen) {\n   613→            throw new IllegalStateException(\"Should have swallowed exception.\", cannotHappen);\n   614→        }\n   615→    }\n   616→\n   617→    private void cleanRemovedTasksCalledByCleanerThread(final long cleanupDelayMs) {\n   618→        for (final TaskDirectory taskDir : listAllTaskDirectories()) {\n   619→            final String dirName = taskDir.file().getName();\n   620→            final TaskId id = parseTaskDirectoryName(dirName, taskDir.namedTopology());\n   621→            if (!lockedTasksToOwner.containsKey(id)) {\n   622→                try {\n   623→                    if (lock(id)) {\n   624→                        final {\n   627→                            removeTaskOffsets(id);\n   628→                            log.info(\"{} Deleting obsolete state directory {} for task {} as {}ms has elapsed (cleanup delay is {}ms).\",\n   629→                                logPrefix(), dirName, id, now - lastModifiedMs, cleanupDelayMs);\n   630→                            removeStartupState(id);\n   631→                            Utils.delete(taskDir.file());\n   632→                        }\n   633→                    }\n   634→                } catch (final IOException exception) {\n   635→                    log.warn(\n   636→                        String.format(\"%s Swallowed the following exception during deletion of obsolete state directory %s for task %s:\",\n   637→                            logPrefix(), dirName, id),\n   638→                        exception\n   639→                    );\n   640→                } finally {\n   641→                    unlock(id);\n   642→                }\n   643→            }\n   644→        }\n   645→        // Ok to ignore returned exception as it should be swallowed\n   646→        maybeCleanEmptyNamedTopologyDirs(true);\n   647→    }\n   648→\n  652→     *                       if false, an exception will be logged as error\n   653→     * @return the first IOException to be encountered\n   654→     */\n   655→    private IOException maybeCleanEmptyNamedTopologyDirs(final boolean logExceptionAsWarn) {\n   656→        if (!hasNamedTopologies) {\n   657→            return null;\n   658→        }\n   659→\n   660→        final pathname.isDirectory() && NAMED_TOPOLOGY_DIR_PATH_NAME.matcher(pathname.getName()).matches()\n   663→        );\n   664→        if (namedTopologyDirs != null) {\n   665→            for (final File Utils.delete(namedTopologyDir);\n   673→                    } catch (final IOException exception) {\n   674→                        if (logExceptionAsWarn) {\n   675→ firstException.compareAndSet(null, exception);\n   688→                    }\n   689→                }\n   690→            }\n   691→        }\n   692→        return firstException.get();\n   693→    }\n   694→\n   695→    /**\n   696→     * Clears out any local state found for the given NamedTopology after it was removed\n   697→     *\n   698→     * @throws StreamsException if cleanup failed\n   699→     */\n   700→    public void clearLocalStateForNamedTopology(final String topologyName) {\n   701→        final File namedTopologyDir = new File(stateDir, getNamedTopologyDirName(topologyName));\n   702→        if (!namedTopologyDir.exists() || !namedTopologyDir.isDirectory()) {\n   703→            log.debug(\"Tried to clear out the local state for topologyName,\n   712→                                       e, new TaskId(-1, -1, topologyName)); // use dummy taskid to report source topology for this error\n   713→        }\n   714→    }\n   715→\n   716→    private void cleanStateAndTaskDirectoriesCalledByUser() throws Exception {\n   717→        if (!lockedTasksToOwner.isEmpty()) {\n   718→            log.warn(\"Found some still-locked task directories when user requested to cleaning up the state, \"\n   719→                + \"since Streams is not running any more these will be ignored to complete the cleanup\");\n   720→        }\n   721→        taskOffsetSums.clear();\n   722→        final AtomicReference<Exception> firstException = new AtomicReference<>();\n   723→        for (final TaskDirectory taskDir : listAllTaskDirectories()) {\n   724→            final String dirName = taskDir.file().getName();\n   725→            final TaskId id = parseTaskDirectoryName(dirName, taskDir.namedTopology());\n           exception\n   740→                );\n   741→                firstException.compareAndSet(null, exception);\n   742→            }\n   743→        }\n   744→\n   745→ TaskExecutorThread extends Thread implements ProcessingThread {\n    43→\n    44→        private final AtomicBoolean shutdownRequested = new AtomicBoolean(false);\n    45→        private final AtomicReference<KafkaFutureImpl<StreamTask>> taskReleaseRequested = new AtomicReference<>(null);\n    46→\n    47→        private final Logger log;\n    48→\n    49→        public TaskExecutorThread(final String name) {\n    50→            super(name);\n    51→            final String logPrefix = String.format(\"%s \", name);\n    52→            final LogContext logContext = new LogContext(logPrefix);\n    53→            log = logContext.logger(DefaultTaskExecutor.class);\n    54→        }\n    55→\n    56→        @Override\n    57→        public void run() {\n    58→            log.info(\"Task executor thread started\");\n    59→            try {\n    60→                while (!shutdownRequested.get()) {\n    61→                    try {\n    62→                        runOnce(time.milliseconds());\n    63→                    } catch (final StreamsException e) {\n    64→                        handleException(e);\n    65→                    } catch (final Exception e) {\n    66→                        handleException(new StreamsException(e));\n    67→                    }\n    68→                }\n    69→            } finally {\n    70→                if (currentTask != null) {\n    71→                    log.debug(\"Releasing task {} due to shutdown.\", currentTask.id());\n    72→                    unassignCurrentTask();\n    73→                }\n    74→\n    75→                final KafkaFutureImpl<StreamTask> taskReleaseFuture;\n    76→                if ((taskReleaseFuture = taskReleaseRequested.getAndSet(null)) != null) {\n    77→                    log.debug(\"Asked to return current task, but shutting down.\");\n    78→                    taskReleaseFuture.complete(null);\n    79→                }\n    80→                log.info(\"Task executor thread shutdown\");\n    81→            }\n    82→        }\n    83→\n    84→        private void handleTaskReleaseRequested() {\n    85→            final KafkaFutureImpl<StreamTask> taskReleaseFuture;\n    86→            if ((taskReleaseFuture = taskReleaseRequested.getAndSet(null)) != null) {\n    87→                if (currentTask != null) {\n    88→                    log.debug(\"Releasing task {} upon request.\", currentTask.id());\n    89→                    final StreamTask unassignedTask = unassignCurrentTask();\n    90→                    taskReleaseFuture.complete(unassignedTask);\n    91→                } else {\n    92→                    log.debug(\"Asked to return current task, but returned current task already.\");\n    93→                    taskReleaseFuture.complete(null);\n    94→                }\n    95→            }\n    96→        }\n    97→\n    98→        private void handleException(final StreamsException e) {\n    99→            if (currentTask != null) {\n   100→                taskManager.setUncaughtException(e, currentTask.id());\n   101→\n   102→                log.debug(\"Releasing task {} due to uncaught exception.\", currentTask.id());\n   103→                unassignCurrentTask();\n   104→            } else {\n   105→                // If we do not currently have a task assigned and still get an error, this is fatal for the executor thread\n   106→                throw e;\n   107→            }\n   108→        }\n   109→\n   110→        private void runOnce(final long nowMs) {\n   111→            handleTaskReleaseRequested();\n   112→\n   113→            if (currentTask == null) {\n   114→                currentTask = taskManager.assignNextTask(DefaultTaskExecutor.this);\n   115→            }\n   116→\n   117→            if (currentTask == null) {\n   118→                try {\n   119→                    taskManager.awaitProcessableTasks(shutdownRequested::get);\n   120→                } catch (final InterruptedException ignored) {\n   121→                    // Can be ignored, the cause of the interrupted will be handled in the event loop\n   122→                }\n   123→            } else {\n   124→                boolean progressed = false;\n   125→\n   126→                if (taskExecutionMetadata.canProcessTask(currentTask, nowMs) && currentTask.isProcessable(nowMs)) {\n   127→                    if (processTask(currentTask, nowMs, time)) {\n   128→                        log.trace(\"processed a record for {}\", currentTask.id());\n   129→                        progressed = true;\n   130→                    }\n   131→                }\n   132→\n   133→                if (taskExecutionMetadata.canPunctuateTask(currentTask)) {\n   134→                    if (currentTask.maybePunctuateStreamTime()) {\n   135→                        log.trace(\"punctuated stream time for task {} \", currentTask.id());\n   136→                        progressed = true;\n The code attempts to mitigate this with a re-check at line 169 (`if (!isShuttingDown.get())`), but there's still a window.\n2. **Signal During Lock Release:** Lines 210, 286, 302 all call `signalAll()` while holding the lock, which is correct, but the condition check before awaiting (lines 156-163) could become stale.\n\n---\n\n### 2. **TopologyMetadata.java** - DEADLOCK RISK WITH MULTIPLE LOCKS\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/TopologyMetadata.java`\n\n**Synchronization Primitives:**\n- `ReentrantLock topologyLock` (line 88)\n- `Condition topologyCV` (line 89) - created from topologyLock\n- Lock/unlock helper methods (lines 152-158)\n\n**Lock Ordering & Patterns:**\n- `version.topologyLock.lock()` at lines 184, 286, 334\n- `version.topologyCV.await()` at line 270\n- `version.topologyCV.signalAll()` at line 223 (inside lock), line 292 (inside lock)\n\n**Potential Issues:**\n1. **Callback Invocation Under Lock (line 293):** `notifyLifecycleObservers()` is called while holding `topologyLock`. If an observer tries to acquire another lock or calls code that requires the lock, deadlock could occur.\n2. **Wait Condition Inside Lock (line 270):** In `maybeWaitForNonEmptyTopology()`, the code waits on `topologyCV` while holding `topologyLock`. While correct for condition variables, if the signaling thread holds another lock that the waiter needs, deadlock can occur.\n\n---\n\n### 3. **CachingKeyValueStore.java** - NESTED LOCK WITH WRITE/READ LOCK DEADLOCK RISK\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/state/internals/CachingKeyValueStore.java`\n\n**Synchronization Primitives:**\n- `ReadWriteLock lock = new ReentrantReadWriteLock()` (line 66)\n- Multiple uses of `lock.writeLock()` and `lock.readLock()`\n\n**Lock Ordering & Patterns:**\n- `lock.writeLock().lock()` at lines 265, 303, 319, 335, 465, 478, 490, 501\n- `lock.readLock().lock()` at lines 147, 453\n- Thread-aware locking at lines 354-367 (get method)\n\n**Critical Issue - NESTED LOCK PROBLEM (lines 317-330):**\n```java\npublic void putAll(final List<KeyValue<Bytes, byte[]>> entries) {\n    validateStoreOpen();\n    lock.writeLock().lock();    // OUTER lock\n    try {\n        validateStoreOpen();\n        for (final KeyValue<Bytes, byte[]> entry : entries) {\n            Objects.requireNonNull(entry.key, \"key cannot be null\");\n            put(entry.key, entry.value);  // CALLS put() which acquires lock AGAIN\n        }\n    } finally {\n        lock.writeLock().unlock();\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**DEADLOCK POTENTIAL:** The `putAll()` method holds `writeLock` and then calls `put()` which tries to acquire `writeLock` again at line 265. While ReentrantReadWriteLock prevents this from deadlocking within the same thread, this is problematic code structure.\n\n**Additional Deadlock Risk (lines 354-367):**\n```java\npublic byte[] get(final Bytes key) {\n    final Lock theLock;\n    if (Thread.currentThread().equals(streamThread)) {\n        theLock = lock.writeLock();  // Gets write lock for stream thread\n    } else {\n        theLock = lock.readLock();   // Gets read lock for other threads\n    }\n    theLock.lock();\n    ...\n}\n```\nThis adaptive locking based on thread identity could cause write-lock starvation if many read threads are queued and the stream thread needs to acquire the write lock.\n\n---\n\n### 4. **StreamThread.java** - WAIT/NOTIFY WITH INTERRUPTION HANDLING\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/StreamThread.java`\n\n**Synchronization Primitives:**\n- `Object stateLock` (implicit from synchronized blocks)\n- `stateLock.wait()` at line 1079\n- `stateLock.notifyAll()` at line 283\n\n**Potential Issues:**\n1. **Lost Wakeup (lines 1069-1100):** The `waitOnThreadState()` method uses spurious wakeup protection with a while loop, which is correct. However:\n   - Line 1076: Timeout logic uses `>=` when it should potentially handle zero timeouts differently\n   - Line 1087: Time calculation with `time.milliseconds()` could miss signals between checks\n\n---\n\n### 5. **KafkaStreams.java** - COMPLEX STATE MACHINE WITH NOTIFY\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/KafkaStreams.java`\n\n**Synchronization Primitives:**\n- `Object stateLock` (implicit from synchronized methods)\n- `stateLock.wait()` at line 311\n- `stateLock.notifyAll()` at line 373\n\n**Lock Ordering:**\n- Line 1118: `synchronized (changeThreadCount)` \n- Line 1130: `synchronized (stateLock)` - POTENTIAL DEADLOCK if reversed elsewhere\n\n**Potential Issues:**\n1. **Lock Ordering Inconsistency (lines 1118-1137):** Acquires `changeThreadCount` lock first, then `stateLock`. Need to verify this order is consistent throughout the codebase.\n2. **Missing Spurious Wakeup Loop (lines 307-315):** The `waitOnStates()` method uses a while loop which is correct, but the timeout could expire prematurely if system time adjusts.\n\n---\n\n### 6. **StateDirectory.java** - MIXED SYNCHRONIZATION PRIMITIVES\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/StateDirectory.java`\n\n**Synchronization Primitives:**\n- `Object taskDirCreationLock` (line 115)\n- `synchronized` methods: `lock()` (line 500), `unlock()` (line 527), `removeStartupState()` (line 286), `clean()` (line 565), `cleanRemovedTasks()` (line 609)\n- `ConcurrentHashMap lockedTasksToOwner` (line 122)\n\n**Potential Issues:**\n1. **Mixed Sync Pattern (lines 500-522):** The `lock()` method is synchronized on `this`, but it accesses `lockedTasksToOwner` which is a ConcurrentHashMap. The put() operation at line 519 is not atomic with the containsKey check at line 505.\n2. **Check-Then-Act Race (lines 505-514):**\n```java\nsynchronized boolean lock(final TaskId taskId) {\n    final Thread lockOwner = lockedTasksToOwner.get(taskId);\n    if (lockOwner != null) {\n        if (lockOwner.equals(Thread.currentThread())) {\n            return true;  // Cached lock\n        } else {\n            return false;  // Another thread owns it\n        }\n    } else {  // RACE: Another thread could insert here\n        lockedTasksToOwner.put(taskId, Thread.currentThread());\n        return true;\n    }\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 7. **DefaultStateUpdater.java & GlobalStreamThread.java** - CONDITION VARIABLE USAGE\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/DefaultStateUpdater.java`\n\n**Synchronization:** Uses Condition variables from ReentrantLock (similar pattern to DefaultTaskManager)\n\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/GlobalStreamThread.java` (lines 454-460)\n\n**Potential Issues:**\n- `initializationLatch.await()` called during `start()` method - if initialization fails, threads may hang\n- Callback functions (TopologyLifecycleObserver) invoked under locks could create deadlock chains\n\n---\n\n## MEDIUM-PRIORITY CONCURRENCY ISSUES\n\n### 8. **PunctuationSchedule.java** - UNSYNCHRONIZED STATE\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/PunctuationSchedule.java`\n\n**Issue (line 26, 62, 66):**\n```java\nprivate boolean isCancelled = false; Here is my complete report on files using synchronization primitives and potential concurrency issues.\n\n### SUMMARY\n- **Total Java files examined:** 728\n- **Files using synchronization primitives:** 52\n- **Critical areas:** Multiple locks, wait/notify patterns, potential deadlocks, missed signals\n\n---\n\n## HIGH-PRIORITY CONCURRENCY ISSUES\n\n### 1. **DefaultTaskManager.java** - POTENTIAL MISSED SIGNALS\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/tasks/DefaultTaskManager.java`\n\n**Synchronization Primitives:**\n- `ReentrantLock tasksLock` (line 62)\n- `Condition tasksCondition` (line 63) - created from tasksLock\n\n**Lock Ordering & Patterns:**\n- `tasksCondition.await()` at line 171\n- `tasksCondition.signalAll()` at lines 192, 210, 286, 302\n\n**Potential Issues:**\n1. **Lost Wakeup Risk (lines 154-188):** In `awaitProcessableTasks()`, after the method releases the lock and a signal occurs, the thread may miss it if it hasn't reached the await yet. The code attempts to mitigate this with a re-check at line 169 (`if (!isShuttingDown.get())`), but there's still a window.\n2. **Signal During Lock Release:** Lines 210, 286, 302 all call `signalAll()` while holding the lock, which is correct, but the condition check before awaiting (lines 156-163) could become stale.\n\n---\n\n### 2. **TopologyMetadata.java** - DEADLOCK RISK WITH MULTIPLE LOCKS\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/TopologyMetadata.java`\n\n**Synchronization Primitives:**\n- `ReentrantLock topologyLock` (line 88)\n- `Condition topologyCV` (line 89) - created from topologyLock\n- Lock/unlock helper methods (lines 152-158)\n\n**Lock Ordering & Patterns:**\n- `version.topologyLock.lock()` at lines 184, 286, 334\n- `version.topologyCV.await()` at line 270\n- `version.topologyCV.signalAll()` at line 223 (inside lock), line 292 (inside lock)\n\n**Potential Issues:**\n1. **Callback Invocation Under Lock (line 293):** `notifyLifecycleObservers()` is called while holding `topologyLock`. If an observer tries to acquire another lock or calls code that requires the lock, deadlock could occur.\n2. **Wait Condition Inside Lock (line 270):** In `maybeWaitForNonEmptyTopology()`, the code waits on `topologyCV` while holding `topologyLock`. While correct for condition variables, if the signaling thread holds another lock that the waiter needs, deadlock can occur.\n\n---\n\n### 3. **CachingKeyValueStore.java** - NESTED LOCK WITH WRITE/READ LOCK DEADLOCK RISK\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/state/internals/CachingKeyValueStore.java`\n\n**Synchronization Primitives:**\n- `ReadWriteLock lock = new ReentrantReadWriteLock()` (line 66)\n- Multiple uses of `lock.writeLock()` and `lock.readLock()`\n\n**Lock Ordering & Patterns:**\n- `lock.writeLock().lock()` at lines 265, 303, 319, 335, 465, 478, 490, 501\n- `lock.readLock().lock()` at lines 147, 453\n- Thread-aware locking at lines 354-367 (get method)\n\n**Critical Issue - NESTED LOCK PROBLEM (lines 317-330):**\n```java\npublic void putAll(final List<KeyValue<Bytes, byte[]>> entries) {\n    validateStoreOpen();\n    lock.writeLock().lock();    // OUTER lock\n    try {\n        validateStoreOpen();\n        for (final KeyValue<Bytes, byte[]> entry : entries) {\n            Objects.requireNonNull(entry.key, \"key cannot be null\");\n            put(entry.key, entry.value);  // CALLS put() which acquires lock AGAIN\n        }\n    } finally {\n        lock.writeLock().unlock();\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**DEADLOCK POTENTIAL:** The `putAll()` method holds `writeLock` and then calls `put()` which tries to acquire `writeLock` again at line 265. While ReentrantReadWriteLock prevents this from deadlocking within the same thread, this is problematic code structure.\n\n**Additional Deadlock Risk (lines 354-367):**\n```java\npublic byte[] get(final Bytes key) {\n    final Lock theLock;\n    if (Thread.currentThread().equals(streamThread)) {\n        theLock = lock.writeLock();  // Gets write lock for stream thread\n    } else {\n        theLock = lock.readLock();   // Gets read lock for other threads\n    }\n    theLock.lock();\n    ...\n}\n```\nThis adaptive locking based on thread identity could cause write-lock starvation if many read threads are queued and the stream thread needs to acquire the write lock.\n\n---\n\n### 4. **StreamThread.java** - WAIT/NOTIFY WITH INTERRUPTION HANDLING\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/StreamThread.java`\n\n**Synchronization Primitives:**\n- `Object stateLock` (implicit from synchronized blocks)\n- `stateLock.wait()` at line 1079\n- `stateLock.notifyAll()` at line 283\n\n**Potential Issues:**\n1. **Lost Wakeup (lines 1069-1100):** The `waitOnThreadState()` method uses spurious wakeup protection with a while loop, which is correct. However:\n   - Line 1076: Timeout logic uses `>=` when it should potentially handle zero timeouts differently\n   - Line 1087: Time calculation with `time.milliseconds()` could miss signals between checks\n\n---\n\n### 5. **KafkaStreams.java** - COMPLEX STATE MACHINE WITH NOTIFY\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/KafkaStreams.java`\n\n**Synchronization Primitives:**\n- `Object stateLock` (implicit from synchronized methods)\n- `stateLock.wait()` at line 311\n- `stateLock.notifyAll()` at line 373\n\n**Lock Ordering:**\n- Line 1118: `synchronized (changeThreadCount)` \n- Line 1130: `synchronized (stateLock)` - POTENTIAL DEADLOCK if reversed elsewhere\n\n**Potential Issues:**\n1. **Lock Ordering Inconsistency (lines 1118-1137):** Acquires `changeThreadCount` lock first, then `stateLock`. Need to verify this order is consistent throughout the codebase.\n2. **Missing Spurious Wakeup Loop (lines 307-315):** The `waitOnStates()` method uses a while loop which is correct, but the timeout could expire prematurely if system time adjusts.\n\n---\n\n### 6. **StateDirectory.java** - MIXED SYNCHRONIZATION PRIMITIVES\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/StateDirectory.java`\n\n**Synchronization Primitives:**\n- `Object taskDirCreationLock` (line 115)\n- `synchronized` methods: `lock()` (line 500), `unlock()` (line 527), `removeStartupState()` (line 286), `clean()` (line 565), `cleanRemovedTasks()` (line 609)\n- `ConcurrentHashMap lockedTasksToOwner` (line 122)\n\n**Potential Issues:**\n1. **Mixed Sync Pattern (lines 500-522):** The `lock()` method is synchronized on `this`, but it accesses `lockedTasksToOwner` which is a ConcurrentHashMap. The put() operation at line 519 is not atomic with the containsKey check at line 505.\n2. **Check-Then-Act Race (lines 505-514):**\n```java\nsynchronized boolean lock(final TaskId taskId) {\n    final Thread lockOwner = lockedTasksToOwner.get(taskId);\n    if (lockOwner != null) {\n        if (lockOwner.equals(Thread.currentThread())) {\n            return true;  // Cached lock\n        } else {\n            return false;  // Another thread owns it\n        }\n    } else {  // RACE: Another thread could insert here\n        lockedTasksToOwner.put(taskId, Thread.currentThread());\n        return true;\n    }\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 7. **DefaultStateUpdater.java & GlobalStreamThread.java** - CONDITION VARIABLE USAGE\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/DefaultStateUpdater.java`\n\n**Synchronization:** Uses Condition variables from ReentrantLock (similar pattern to DefaultTaskManager)\n\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/GlobalStreamThread.java` (lines 454-460)\n\n**Potential Issues:**\n- `initializationLatch.await()` called during `start()` method - if initialization fails, threads may hang\n- Callback functions (TopologyLifecycleObserver) invoked under locks could create deadlock chains\n\n---\n\n## MEDIUM-PRIORITY CONCURRENCY ISSUES\n\n### 8. **PunctuationSchedule.java** - UNSYNCHRONIZED STATE\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/PunctuationSchedule.java`\n\n**Issue (line 26, 62, 66):**\n```java\nprivate boolean isCancelled = false;  // NOT volatile or synchronized\nboolean isCancelled() {\n    return isCancelled;  // Unsynchronized read\n}\n```\nWhile `RepointableCancellable.setSchedule()` and `cancel()` are synchronized, the `isCancelled()` method on the main class is not, creating potential visibility issues.\n\n---\n\n### 9. **ThreadCache.java** - SYNCHRONIZED METHOD WITH NESTED LOCK\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/state/internals/ThreadCache.java`\n\n**Issue (lines 80-101):**\n```java\npublic synchronized void resize(final long newCacheSizeBytes) {\n    // ...\n    while (sizeInBytes.get() > maxCacheSizeBytes) {\n        final NamedCache cache = circularIterator.next();\n        synchronized (cache) {  // NESTED synchronization\n            // ...\n        }\n    }\n}\n```\nHolding the ThreadCache lock while acquiring individual NamedCache locks could cause deadlock if operations are performed in reverse order elsewhere.\n\n---\n\n### 10. **SynchronizedPartitionGroup.java** - SYNCHRONIZED WRAPPER\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/SynchronizedPartitionGroup.java`\n\n**Issue:** All methods are synchronized on `this`, but the wrapped object may not be thread-safe. If any method on wrapped calls back to a synchronized method, recursive synchronization occurs.\n\n---\n\n### 11. **PunctuationQueue.java** - LOCK ON MUTABLE OBJECT\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/PunctuationQueue.java`\n\n**Issue (lines 30, 37, 46, 74):**\n```java\nsynchronized (pq) {  // pq is the PriorityQueue instance\n    // ...\n}\n```\nSynchronizing on a mutable collection is fragile. If the PriorityQueue is reassigned, synchronization semantics break.\n\n---\n\n### 12. **StreamsMetadataState.java** - MULTIPLE SYNCHRONIZED METHODS\n**File:** `/app/streams/src/main/java/org/apache/kafka/streams/processor/internals/StreamsMetadataState.java`\n\n**Issue:** Multiple synchronized methods (lines 112, 149, 173, 206, 223, 247, 282, 311) on the same object. If callback within these methods acquires other locks, deadlock is possible.\n\n---\n\n## SUMMARY TABLE\n\n| File | Lock Type | Lock Ordering | Wait/Notify | Risk Level | Issue private void validateTopologyConsistency(final Set<String> activeTopologies) {\n   110→        executeWithTasksLocked(() -> {\n   111→            for (final StreamTask task :
