java.util.Objects;\n    48→import java.util.Optional;\n    49→import java.util.Set;\n    50→import java.util.concurrent.ConcurrentHashMap;\n    51→import java.util.concurrent.ConcurrentNavigableMap;\n    52→import java.util.concurrent.ConcurrentSkipListMap;\n    53→import java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicLong;\n    54→import java.util.concurrent.locks.Condition;\n    55→import java.util.concurrent.locks.ReentrantLock;\n    56→import java.util.function.Consumer;\n    57→import java.util.function.Function;\n    58→import java.util.function.Supplier;\n    59→import java.util.regex.Pattern;\n    60→import java.util.stream.Collectors;\n    61→\n    62→import static java.util.Collections.emptySet;\n    63→\n    64→public class TopologyMetadata {\n    65→    private Logger log;\n    66→\n    67→    // the \"__\" (double underscore) string is not allowed for topology names, so it's safe to use to indicate\n    68→    // that it's not a named topology\n    69→    public static final String UNNAMED_TOPOLOGY = \"__UNNAMED_TOPOLOGY__\";\n    70→    private static final Pattern EMPTY_ZERO_LENGTH_PATTERN = Pattern.compile(\"\");\n    71→\n    72→    private final StreamsConfig config;\n    73→    private final ProcessingMode processingMode;\n    74→    private final TopologyVersion version;\n    75→    private final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata;\n    76→    private final Set<String> pausedTopologies;\n    77→\n    78→    private final ConcurrentNavigableMap<String, InternalTopologyBuilder> builders; // Keep sorted by topology name for readability\n    79→    private final List<TopologyLifecycleObserver> lifecycleObservers = new ArrayList<>();\n    80→\n    81→    private ProcessorTopology globalTopology;\n    82→    private final Map<String, StateStore> globalStateStores = new HashMap<>();\n    83→    private final Set<String> allInputTopics = new HashSet<>();\n    84→    private final Map<String, Long> threadVersions = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();\n    85→\n    86→    public static class TopologyVersion {\n    87→        public AtomicLong topologyVersion = new AtomicLong(0L); // the local topology version\n    88→        public ReentrantLock topologyLock = new ReentrantLock();\n    89→        public Condition topologyCV = topologyLock.newCondition();\n    90→        public List<TopologyVersionListener> activeTopologyUpdateListeners = new LinkedList<>();\n    91→    }\n    92→\n    93→    public static class TopologyVersionListener {\n    94→        final long topologyVersion; // the (minimum) version to wait for these threads to cross\n    95→        final KafkaFutureImpl<Void> future; // the future waiting on all threads to be updated\n    96→\n    97→        public TopologyVersionListener(final long topologyVersion, final KafkaFutureImpl<Void> future) {\n    98→            this.topologyVersion = topologyVersion;\n    99→            this.future = future;\n   100→        }\n   101→    }\n   102→\n   103→    public interface TopologyLifecycleObserver {\n   104→        void onTopologyChange(long newVersion, Set<String> activeTopologies);\n   105→    }\n   106→\n   107→    public TopologyMetadata(final InternalTopologyBuilder builder,\n   108→                            final StreamsConfig config) {\n   109→        this.version = new TopologyVersion();\n   110→        this.processingMode = StreamsConfigUtils.processingMode(config);\n   111→        this.config = config;\n   112→        this.log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(getClass());\n   113→        this.pausedTopologies = ConcurrentHashMap.newKeySet();\n   114→\n   115→        builders = new ConcurrentSkipListMap<>();\n   116→        if (builder.hasNamedTopology()) {\n   117→            builders.put(builder.topologyName(), builder);\n   118→        } else {\n   119→            builders.put(UNNAMED_TOPOLOGY, builder);\n   120→        }\n   121→        this.taskExecutionMetadata = new TaskExecutionMetadata(builders.keySet(), pausedTopologies, processingMode);\n   122→    }\n   123→\n   124→    public TopologyMetadata(final ConcurrentNavigableMap<String, InternalTopologyBuilder> builders,\n   125→                            final StreamsConfig config) {\n   126→        this.version = new TopologyVersion();\n   127→        this.processingMode = StreamsConfigUtils.processingMode(config);\n   128→        this.config = config;\n   129→        this.log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(getClass());\n   130→        this.pausedTopologies = ConcurrentHashMap.newKeySet();\n   131→\n   132→        this.builders = builders;\n   133→        if (builders.isEmpty()) {\n   134→            log.info(\"Created an empty KafkaStreams app with no topology\");\n   135→        }\n   136→        this.taskExecutionMetadata = new TaskExecutionMetadata(builders.keySet(), pausedTopologies, processingMode);\n   137→    }\n   138→\n   139→    // Need to (re)set the log here to pick up the `processId` part of the clientId in the prefix\n   140→    public void setLog(final LogContext logContext) {\n   141→        log = logContext.logger(getClass());\n   142→    }\n   143→    \n   144→    public ProcessingMode processingMode() {\n   145→        return processingMode;\n   146→    }\n   147→\n   148→    public long topologyVersion() {\n   149→        return version.topologyVersion.get();\n   150→    }\n   151→\n   152→    private void lock() {\n   153→        version.topologyLock.lock();\n   154→    }\n   155→\n   156→    private void unlock() {\n   157→        version.topologyLock.unlock();\n   158→    }\n   159→\n   160→    public Collection<String> sourceTopicsForTopology(final String name) {\n   161→        return builders.get(name).fullSourceTopicNames();\n   162→    }\n   163→\n   164→    public boolean needsUpdate(final String threadName) {\n   165→        return threadVersions.get(threadName) < topologyVersion();\n   166→    }\n   167→\n   168→    public void registerThread(final String threadName) {\n   169→        threadVersions.put(threadName, 0L);\n   170→    }\n   171→\n   172→    public void unregisterThread(final String threadName) {\n   173→        threadVersions.remove(threadName);\n   174→        maybeNotifyTopologyVersionListeners();\n   175→    }\n   176→\n   177→    public TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata() {\n   178→        return taskExecutionMetadata;\n   179→    }\n   180→\n   181→    public void executeTopologyUpdatesAndBumpThreadVersion(final Consumer<Set<String>> handleTopologyAdditions,\n   182→                                                           final Consumer<Set<String>> handleTopologyRemovals) {\n   183→        try {\n   184→            version.topologyLock.lock();\n   185→            final long latestTopologyVersion = topologyVersion();\n   186→            handleTopologyAdditions.accept(namedTopologiesView());\n   187→            handleTopologyRemovals.accept(namedTopologiesView());\n   188→            threadVersions.put(Thread.currentThread().getName(), latestTopologyVersion);\n   189→        } finally {\n   190→            version.topologyLock.unlock();\n   191→        }\n   192→    }\n   193→\n   194→    public void maybeNotifyTopologyVersionListeners() {\n   195→        try {\n   196→            lock();\n   197→            final long minThreadVersion = minimumThreadVersion();\n   198→            final Iterator<TopologyVersionListener> iterator = version.activeTopologyUpdateListeners.listIterator();\n   199→            TopologyVersionListener topologyVersionListener;\n   200→            while (iterator.hasNext()) {\n   201→                topologyVersionListener = iterator.next();\n   202→                final long topologyVersionWaitersVersion = topologyVersionListener.topologyVersion;\n   203→                if (minThreadVersion >= topologyVersionWaitersVersion) {\n   204→                    topologyVersionListener.future.complete(null);\n   205→                    iterator.remove();\n   206→                    log.info(\"All threads are now on topology version {}\", topologyVersionListener.topologyVersion);\n   207→                }\n   208→            }\n   209→        } finally {\n   210→            unlock();\n   211→        }\n   212→    }\n   213→\n   214→    // Return the minimum version across all live threads, or Long.MAX_VALUE if there are no threads running\n   215→    private long minimumThreadVersion() {\n   216→        final Optional<Long> minVersion = threadVersions.values().stream().min(Long::compare);\n   217→        return minVersion.orElse(Long.MAX_VALUE);\n   218→    }\n   219→\n   220→    public void wakeupThreads() {\n   221→        try {\n   222→            lock();\n   223→            version.topologyCV.signalAll();\n   224→        } finally {\n   225→            unlock();\n   226→        }\n   227→    }\n   228→\n   229→    public void registerLifecycleObserver(final TopologyLifecycleObserver observer) {\n   230→        lock();\n   231→        try {\n   232→            lifecycleObservers.add(observer);\n   233→        } finally {\n   234→            unlock();\n   235→        }\n   236→    }\n   237→\n   238→    private void notifyLifecycleObservers() {\n   239→        final long currentVersion = version.topologyVersion.get();\n   240→        final Set<String> activeTopologies = namedTopologiesView();\n   241→        for (final TopologyLifecycleObserver observer : lifecycleObservers) {\n   242→            try {\n   243→                observer.onTopologyChange(currentVersion, activeTopologies);\n   244→            } catch (final Exception e) {\n   245→                log.warn(\"Topology lifecycle observer threw exception\", e);\n   246→            }\n   247→        }\n   248→    }\n   249→\n   250→    public boolean isTaskTopologyStale(final TaskId taskId) {\n   251→        lock();\n   252→        try {\n   253→            final String topologyName = taskId.topologyName();\n   254→            if (topologyName != null) {\n   255→                return !builders.containsKey(topologyName);\n   256→            }\n   257→            return false;\n   258→        } finally {\n   259→            unlock();\n   260→        }\n   261→    }\n   262→\n   263→    public void maybeWaitForNonEmptyTopology(final Supplier<StreamThread.State> threadState) {\n   264→        if (isEmpty() && threadState.get().isAlive()) {\n   265→            try {\n   266→                lock();\n   267→                while (isEmpty() && threadState.get().isAlive()) {\n   268→                    try {\n   269→                        log.debug(\"Detected that the topology is currently empty, waiting for something to process\");\n   270→                        version.topologyCV.await();\n   271→                    } catch (final InterruptedException e) {\n   272→                        log.error(\"StreamThread was interrupted while waiting on empty topology\", e);\n   273→                    }\n   274→                }\n   275→            } finally {\n   276→                unlock();\n   277→            }\n   278→        }\n   279→    }\n   280→\n   281→    /**\n   282→     * Adds the topology and registers a future that listens for all threads on the older version to see the update\n   283→     */\n   284→    public void registerAndBuildNewTopology(final KafkaFutureImpl<Void> future, final InternalTopologyBuilder newTopologyBuilder) {\n   285→        try {\n   286→            lock();\n   287→            buildAndVerifyTopology(newTopologyBuilder);\n   288→            log.info(\"New NamedTopology {} passed validation and will be added, old topology version is {}\", newTopologyBuilder.topologyName(), version.topologyVersion.get());\n   289→            version.topologyVersion.incrementAndGet();\n   290→            version.activeTopologyUpdateListeners.add(new TopologyVersionListener(topologyVersion(), future));\n   291→            builders.put(newTopologyBuilder.topologyName(), newTopologyBuilder);\n   292→            wakeupThreads();\n   293→            notifyLifecycleObservers();\n   294→            log.info(\"Added NamedTopology {} and updated topology version to {}\", newTopologyBuilder.topologyName(), version.topologyVersion.get());\n   295→        } catch (final Throwable throwable) {\n   296→            log.error(\"Failed to add NamedTopology {}, please retry the operation.\", newTopologyBuilder.topologyName());\n   297→            future.completeExceptionally(throwable);\n   298→        } finally {\n   299→            unlock();\n   300→        }\n   301→    }\n   302→\n   303→    /**\n   304→     * Pauses a topology by name\n   305→     * @param topologyName Name of the topology to pause\n   306→     */\n   307→    public void pauseTopology(final String topologyName) {\n   308→        pausedTopologies.add(topologyName);\n   309→    }\n   310→\n   311→    /**\n   312→     * Checks if a given topology is paused.\n   313→     * @param topologyName If null, assume that we are checking the `UNNAMED_TOPOLOGY`.\n   314→     * @return A boolean indicating if the topology is paused.\n   315→     */\n   316→    public boolean isPaused(final String topologyName) {\n   317→        return pausedTopologies.contains(getTopologyNameOrElseUnnamed(topologyName));\n   318→    }\n   319→\n   320→    /**\n   321→     * Resumes a topology by name\n   322→     * @param topologyName Name of the topology to resume\n   323→     */\n   324→    public void resumeTopology(final String topologyName) {\n   325→        pausedTopologies.remove(topologyName);\n   326→    }\n   327→\n   328→    /**\n   329→     * Removes the topology and registers a future that listens for all threads on the older version to see the update\n   330→     */\n   331→    public KafkaFuture<Void> unregisterTopology(final KafkaFutureImpl<Void> removeTopologyFuture,\n   332→                                                final String topologyName) {\n   333→        try {\n   334→            lock();\n   335→            log.info(\"Beginning removal of NamedTopology {}, old topology version is {}\", topologyName, version.topologyVersion.get());\n   336→            version.topologyVersion.incrementAndGet();\n   337→            version.activeTopologyUpdateListeners.add(new TopologyVersionListener(topologyVersion(), removeTopologyFuture));\n   338→            final InternalTopologyBuilder removedBuilder = builders.remove(topologyName);\n   339→            removedBuilder.fullSourceTopicNames().forEach(allInputTopics::remove);\n   340→            removedBuilder.allSourcePatternStrings().forEach(allInputTopics::remove);\n   341→            notifyLifecycleObservers();\n   342→            log.info(\"Finished removing NamedTopology {}, topology version was updated to {}\", topologyName, version.topologyVersion.get());\n   343→        } catch (final Throwable throwable) {\n   344→            log.error(\"Failed to remove NamedTopology {}, please retry.\", topologyName);\n   345→            removeTopologyFuture.completeExceptionally(throwable);\n   346→        } finally {\n   347→            unlock();\n   348→        }\n   349→        return removeTopologyFuture;\n   350→    }\n   351→\n   352→    public TaskConfig taskConfig(final TaskId taskId) {\n   353→        final InternalTopologyBuilder builder = lookupBuilderForTask(taskId);\n   354→        return builder.topologyConfigs().getTaskConfig();\n   355→    }\n   356→\n   357→    public void buildAndRewriteTopology() {\n   358→        applyToEachBuilder(this::buildAndVerifyTopology);\n   359→    }\n   360→\n   361→    private void buildAndVerifyTopology(final InternalTopologyBuilder builder) {\n   362→        builder.rewriteTopology(config);\n   363→        builder.buildTopology();\n   364→\n   365→        final Set<String> allInputTopicsCopy = new HashSet<>(allInputTopics);\n   366→\n   367→        // As we go, check each topology for overlap in the set of input topics/patterns\n   368→        final int numInputTopics = allInputTopicsCopy.size();\n   369→        final List<String> inputTopics = builder.fullSourceTopicNames();\n   370→        final Collection<String> inputPatterns = builder.allSourcePatternStrings();\n   371→\n   372→        final Set<String> newInputTopics = new HashSet<>(inputTopics);\n   373→        newInputTopics.addAll(inputPatterns);\n   374→\n   375→        final int numNewInputTopics = newInputTopics.size();\n   376→        allInputTopicsCopy.addAll(newInputTopics);\n   377→\n   378→        if (allInputTopicsCopy.size() != numInputTopics + numNewInputTopics) {\n   379→            inputTopics.retainAll(allInputTopicsCopy);\n   380→            inputPatterns.retainAll(allInputTopicsCopy);\n   381→            log.error(\"Tried to add the NamedTopology {} but it had overlap with other input topics {} or patterns {}\",\n   382→                      builder.topologyName(), inputTopics, inputPatterns);\n   383→            throw new TopologyException(\"Named Topologies may not subscribe to the same input topics or patterns\");\n   384→        }\n   385→\n   386→        final ProcessorTopology globalTopology = builder.buildGlobalStateTopology();\n   387→        if (globalTopology != null) {\n   388→            if (builder.topologyName() != null) {\n   389→                throw new IllegalStateException(\"Global state stores are not supported with Named Topologies\");\n   390→            } else if (this.globalTopology != null) {\n   391→                throw new TopologyException(\"Topology builder had global state, but global topology has already been set\");\n   392→            } else {\n   393→                this.globalTopology = globalTopology;\n   394→                globalStateStores.putAll(builder.globalStateStores());\n   395→            }\n   396→        }\n   397→        allInputTopics.addAll(newInputTopics);\n   398→    }\n   399→\n   400→    public int numStreamThreads(final StreamsConfig config) {\n   401→        final int configuredNumStreamThreads = config.getInt(StreamsConfig.NUM_STREAM_THREADS_CONFIG);\n   402→\n   403→        // If there are named topologies but some are empty, this indicates a bug in user code\n   404→        if (hasNamedTopologies()) {\n   405→            if (hasNoLocalTopology()) {\n   406→                log.error(\"Detected a named topology with no input topics, a named topology may not be empty.\");\n   407→                throw new TopologyException(\"Topology has no stream threads and no global threads, \" +\n   408→                                                \"must subscribe to at least one source topic or pattern.\");\n   409→            }\n   410→        } else {\n   411→            // If both the global and non-global topologies are empty, this indicates a bug in user code\n   412→            if (hasNoLocalTopology() && !hasGlobalTopology()) {\n   413→                log.error(\"Topology with no input topics will create no stream threads and no global thread.\");\n   414→                throw new TopologyException(\"Topology has no stream threads and no global threads, \" +\n   415→                                                \"must subscribe to at least one source topic or global table.\");\n   416→            }\n   417→        }\n   418→\n   419→        // Lastly we check for an empty non-global topology and override the threads to zero if set otherwise\n   420→        if (configuredNumStreamThreads != 0 && hasNoLocalTopology()) {\n   421→            log.info(\"Overriding number of StreamThreads to zero for global-only topology\");\n   422→            return 0;\n   423→        }\n   424→\n   425→        return configuredNumStreamThreads;\n   426→    }\n   427→\n   428→    /**\n   429→     * @return true iff the app is using named topologies, or was started up with no topology at all\n   430→     */\n   431→    public boolean hasNamedTopologies() {\n   432→        return !builders.containsKey(UNNAMED_TOPOLOGY);\n   433→    }\n   434→\n   435→    public Set<String> namedTopologiesView() {\n   436→        return hasNamedTopologies() ? Collections.unmodifiableSet(builders.keySet()) : emptySet();\n   437→    }\n   438→\n   439→    /**\n   440→     * @return true iff any of the topologies have a global topology\n   441→     */\n   442→    public boolean hasGlobalTopology() {\n   443→        return evaluateConditionIsTrueForAnyBuilders(InternalTopologyBuilder::hasGlobalStores);\n   444→    }\n   445→\n   446→    /**\n   447→     * @return true iff any of the topologies have no local (aka non-global) topology\n   448→     */\n   449→    public boolean hasNoLocalTopology() {\n   450→        return evaluateConditionIsTrueForAnyBuilders(InternalTopologyBuilder::hasNoLocalTopology);\n   451→    }\n   452→\n   453→    public boolean hasPersistentStores() {\n   454→        // If the app is using named topologies, there may not be any persistent state when it first starts up\n   455→        // but a new NamedTopology may introduce it later, so we must return true\n   456→        if (hasNamedTopologies()) {\n   457→            return true;\n   458→        }\n   459→        return evaluateConditionIsTrueForAnyBuilders(InternalTopologyBuilder::hasPersistentStores);\n   460→    }\n   461→\n   462→    public boolean hasStore(final String name) {\n   463→        return evaluateConditionIsTrueForAnyBuilders(b -> b.hasStore(name));\n   464→    }\n   465→\n   466→    public boolean hasOffsetResetOverrides() {\n   467→        // Return true if using named topologies, as there may be named topologies added later which do have overrides\n   468→        return hasNamedTopologies() || evaluateConditionIsTrueForAnyBuilders(InternalTopologyBuilder::hasOffsetResetOverrides);\n   469→    }\n   470→\n   471→    public Optional<AutoOffsetResetStrategy> offsetResetStrategy(final String topic) {\n   472→        for (final InternalTopologyBuilder builder : builders.values()) {\n   473→            if (builder.containsTopic(topic)) {\n   474→                return Optional.ofNullable(builder.offsetResetStrategy(topic));\n   475→            }\n   476→        }\n   477→        log.warn(\"Unable to look up offset reset strategy for topic {} \" +\n   478→            \"as this topic does not appear in the sources of any of the current topologies: {}\\n \" +\n   479→                \"This may be due to natural race condition when removing a topology but it should not \" +\n   480→                \"persist or appear frequently.\",\n   481→            topic, namedTopologiesView()\n   482→        );\n   483→        // returning `null` for an Optional return type triggers spotbugs\n   484→        // we added an exception for NP_OPTIONAL_RETURN_NULL for this method\n   485→        // when we remove NamedTopologies, we can remove this exception\n   486→        return null;\n   487→    }\n   488→\n   489→\n   490→    public Collection<String> fullSourceTopicNamesForTopology(final String topologyName) {\n   491→        Objects.requireNonNull(topologyName, \"topology name must not be null\");\n   492→        return lookupBuilderForNamedTopology(topologyName).fullSourceTopicNames();\n   493→    }\n   494→\n   495→    public Collection<String> allFullSourceTopicNames() {\n   496→        final List<String> sourceTopics = new ArrayList<>();\n   497→        applyToEachBuilder(b -> sourceTopics.addAll(b.fullSourceTopicNames()));\n   498→        return sourceTopics;\n   499→    }\n   500→\n   501→    Pattern sourceTopicPattern() {\n   502→        final StringBuilder patternBuilder = new StringBuilder();\n   503→\n   504→        applyToEachBuilder(b -> {\n   505→            final String patternString = b.sourceTopicPatternString();\n   506→            if (!patternString.isEmpty()) {\n   507→                patternBuilder.append(patternString).append(\"|\");\n   508→            }\n   509→        });\n   510→\n   511→        if (patternBuilder.length() > 0) {\n   512→            patternBuilder.setLength(patternBuilder.length() - 1);\n   513→            return Pattern.compile(patternBuilder.toString());\n   514→        } else {\n   515→            return EMPTY_ZERO_LENGTH_PATTERN;\n   516→        }\n   517→    }\n   518→\n   519→    public boolean usesPatternSubscription() {\n   520→        return evaluateConditionIsTrueForAnyBuilders(InternalTopologyBuilder::usesPatternSubscription);\n   521→    }\n   522→\n   523→    // Can be empty if app is started up with no Named Topologies, in order to add them on later\n   524→    public boolean isEmpty() {\n   525→        return builders.isEmpty();\n   526→    }\n   527→\n   528→    public String topologyDescriptionString() {\n   529→        if (isEmpty()) {\n   530→            return \"\";\n   531→        }\n   532→        final StringBuilder sb = new StringBuilder();\n   533→\n   534→        applyToEachBuilder(b -> sb.append(b.describe().toString()));\n   535→\n   536→        return sb.toString();\n   537→    }\n   538→\n   539→    /**\n   540→     * @return the {@link ProcessorTopology subtopology} built for this task, guaranteed to be non-null\n   541→     *\n   542→     * @throws UnknownTopologyException  if the task is from a named topology that this client isn't aware of\n   543→     */\n   544→    public ProcessorTopology buildSubtopology(final TaskId task) {\n   545→        final InternalTopologyBuilder builder = lookupBuilderForTask(task);\n   546→        return builder.buildSubtopology(task.subtopology());\n   547→    }\n   548→\n   549→    public ProcessorTopology globalTaskTopology() {\n   550→        if (hasNamedTopologies()) {\n   551→            throw new IllegalStateException(\"Global state stores are not supported with Named Topologies\");\n   552→        }\n   553→        return globalTopology;\n   554→    }\n   555→\n   556→    public Map<String, StateStore> globalStateStores() {\n   557→        return globalStateStores;\n   558→    }\n   559→\n   560→    public Map<String, List<String>>   564→    public Set<String> stateStoreNamesForSubtopology(final String topologyName, final int subtopologyId) {\n   565→        return lookupBuilderForNamedTopology(topologyName).stateStoreNamesForSubtopology(subtopologyId);\n   566→    }\n   567→\n   568→    public Map<String, List<String>> stateStoreNameToSourceTopics() {\n   569→        final Map<String, List<String>> stateStoreNameToSourceTopics = new HashMap<>();\n   570→        applyToEachBuilder(b -> stateStoreNameToSourceTopics.putAll(b.stateStoreNameToFullSourceTopicNames()));\n   571→        return stateStoreNameToSourceTopics;\n   572→    }\n   573→\n   574→    public String storeForChangelogTopic(final String topicName) {\n   575→        for (final InternalTopologyBuilder builder : builders.values()) {\n   576→            final String store = builder.storeForChangelogTopic(topicName);\n   577→            if (store != null) {\n   578→                return store;\n   579→            }\n   580→        }\n   581→        log.warn(\"Unable to locate any store for topic {}\", topicName);\n   582→        return \"\";\n   583→    }\n   584→\n   585→    /**\n   586→     * @param storeName       the name of the state store\n   587→     * @param topologyName    the name of the topology to search for stores within\n   588→     * @return topics subscribed from source processors that are connected to these state stores\n   589→     */\n   590→    public Collection<String> sourceTopicsForStore(final String storeName, final String topologyName) {\n   591→        return lookupBuilderForNamedTopology(topologyName).sourceTopicsForStore(storeName);\n   592→    }\n   593→\n   594→    public static String getTopologyNameOrElseUnnamed(final String topologyName) {\n   595→        return topologyName == null ? UNNAMED_TOPOLOGY : topologyName;\n   596→    }\n   597→\n   598→    /**\n   599→     * @param topologiesToExclude the names of any topologies to exclude from the returned topic groups,\n   600→     *                            eg because they have missing source topics and can't be processed yet\n   601→     *\n   602→     * @return                    flattened map of all subtopologies (from all topologies) to topics info\n   603→     */\n   604→    public Map<Subtopology, TopicsInfo> subtopologyTopicsInfoMapExcluding(final Set<String> topologiesToExclude) {\n   605→        final           subtopologyTopicsInfo.putAll(b.subtopologyToTopicsInfo());\n   609→            }\n   610→        });\n   611→        return subtopologyTopicsInfo;\n   612→    }\n   613→\n   614→    /**\n   618→        final Map<String, Map<Subtopology, TopicsInfo>> topologyToSubtopologyTopicsInfoMap = new HashMap<>();\n   619→        applyToEachBuilder(b -> topologyToSubtopologyTopicsInfoMap.put(b.topologyName(), b.subtopologyToTopicsInfo()));\n   620→        return  topologyToSubtopologyTopicsInfoMap;\n   621→    }\n   622→\n   623→    public 641→    /**\n   642→     * @return the {@link InternalTopologyBuilder} for this task's topology, guaranteed to be non-null\n   643→     *\n   644→     * @throws UnknownTopologyException  if the task is from a named topology that this client isn't aware of\n   645→     */\n   646→    private InternalTopologyBuilder lookupBuilderForTask(final TaskId task) {\n   647→        final == null) {\n   651→            throw new UnknownTopologyException(\"Unable to locate topology builder\", task.topologyName());\n   652→        } else {\n   653→            return builder;\n   654→        }\n   655→    }\n   656→\n   657→    @SuppressWarnings(\"deprecation\")\n   658→    public Collection<NamedTopology> allNamedTopologies() {\n   659→        return builders.values()\n   660→            .stream()\n   661→            .map(InternalTopologyBuilder::namedTopology)\n   662→            .collect(Collectors.toSet());\n   663→    }\n   664→\n   665→\n   666→    /**\n   667→     * @return *         else returns {@code null} if {@code topologyName} is non-null but no such NamedTopology exists\n   670→     */\n   671→    public InternalTopologyBuilder lookupBuilderForNamedTopology(final Function<InternalTopologyBuilder, Boolean> condition) {\n   676→        for (final InternalTopologyBuilder builder : builders.values()) {\n   677→            if (condition.apply(builder)) {\n   678→                return true;\n   679→            }\n   680→        }\n   681→        return false;\n   682→    }\n   683→\n   684→    private void applyToEachBuilder(final Consumer<InternalTopologyBuilder> function) {\n   685→        for (final InternalTopologyBuilder builder : builders.values()) {\n   686→            function.accept(builder);\n   687→        }\n   688→    }\n   689→\n   690→    public static class Subtopology implements Comparable<Subtopology> {\n   691→        final int nodeGroupId;\n   692→        final String namedTopology;\n   693→\n   694→        public Subtopology(final int nodeGroupId, final String namedTopology) {\n   695→            this.nodeGroupId = nodeGroupId;\n   696→            this.namedTopology = namedTopology;\n   697→        }\n   698→\n   699→        @Override\n   700→        public boolean equals(final Object o) {\n   701→            if (this == o) {\n   702→                return true;\n   703→            }\n   704→            if (o == null || getClass() != o.getClass()) {\n   705→                return false;\n   706→            }\n   707→            final Subtopology that = (Subtopology) o;\n   708→            return nodeGroupId == that.nodeGroupId &&\n   709→                    Objects.equals(namedTopology, that.namedTopology);\n   710→        }\n   711→\n   712→        @Override\n   713→        public int hashCode() {\n   714→            return Objects.hash(nodeGroupId, namedTopology);\n   715→        }\n   716→\n   717→        @Override\n   718→        public int compareTo(final Subtopology other) {\n   719→            if (nodeGroupId != other.nodeGroupId) {\n   720→                return Integer.compare(nodeGroupId, other.nodeGroupId);\n   721→            }\n   722→            if (namedTopology == null) {\n   723→                return other.namedTopology == null ? 0 : -1;\n   724→            }\n   725→            if (other.namedTopology == null) {\n   726→                return 1;\n   727→            }\n   728→\n   729→            // Both not null\n   730→            return namedTopology.compareTo(other.namedTopology);\n   731→        }\n   732→    }\n   733→}\n java.util.function.Supplier;\nimport java.util.regex.Pattern;\nimport java.util.stream.Collectors;\n\nimport static java.util.Collections.emptySet;\n\npublic class TopologyMetadata {\n    private Logger log;\n\n    // the \"__\" (double underscore) string is not allowed for topology names, so it's safe to use to indicate\n    // that it's not a named topology\n    public static final String UNNAMED_TOPOLOGY = \"__UNNAMED_TOPOLOGY__\";\n    private static final Pattern EMPTY_ZERO_LENGTH_PATTERN = Pattern.compile(\"\");\n\n    private final StreamsConfig config;\n    private final ProcessingMode processingMode;\n    private final TopologyVersion version;\n    private final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata;\n    private final Set<String> pausedTopologies;\n\n    private final ConcurrentNavigableMap<String, InternalTopologyBuilder> builders; // Keep sorted by topology name for readability\n    private final List<TopologyLifecycleObserver> lifecycleObservers = new ArrayList<>();\n\n    private ProcessorTopology globalTopology;\n    private final Map<String, StateStore> globalStateStores = new HashMap<>();\n    private final Set<String> allInputTopics = new HashSet<>();\n    private final Map<String, Long> threadVersions = new ConcurrentHashMap<>();\n\n    public static class TopologyVersion {\n        public AtomicLong topologyVersion = new AtomicLong(0L); // the local topology version\n        public ReentrantLock topologyLock = new ReentrantLock();\n        public Condition topologyCV = topologyLock.newCondition();\n        public List<TopologyVersionListener> activeTopologyUpdateListeners = new LinkedList<>();\n    }\n\n    public static class TopologyVersionListener {\n        final long topologyVersion; // the (minimum) version to wait for these threads to cross\n        final KafkaFutureImpl<Void> future; // the future waiting on all threads to be updated\n\n        public TopologyVersionListener(final long topologyVersion, final KafkaFutureImpl<Void> future) {\n            this.topologyVersion = topologyVersion;\n            this.future = future;\n        }\n    }\n\n    public interface TopologyLifecycleObserver {\n        void onTopologyChange(long newVersion, Set<String> activeTopologies);\n    }\n\n    public TopologyMetadata(final InternalTopologyBuilder builder,\n                            final StreamsConfig config) {\n        this.version = new TopologyVersion();\n        this.processingMode = StreamsConfigUtils.processingMode(config);\n        this.config = config;\n        this.log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(getClass());\n        this.pausedTopologies = ConcurrentHashMap.newKeySet();\n\n        builders = new ConcurrentSkipListMap<>();\n        if (builder.hasNamedTopology()) {\n            builders.put(builder.topologyName(), builder);\n        } else {\n            builders.put(UNNAMED_TOPOLOGY, builder);\n        }\n        this.taskExecutionMetadata = new TaskExecutionMetadata(builders.keySet(), pausedTopologies, processingMode);\n    }\n\n    public TopologyMetadata(final ConcurrentNavigableMap<String, InternalTopologyBuilder> builders,\n                            final StreamsConfig config) {\n        this.version = new TopologyVersion();\n        this.processingMode = StreamsConfigUtils.processingMode(config);\n        this.config = config;\n        this.log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(getClass());\n        this.pausedTopologies = ConcurrentHashMap.newKeySet();\n\n        this.builders = builders;\n        if (builders.isEmpty()) {\n            log.info(\"Created an empty KafkaStreams app with no topology\");\n        }\n        this.taskExecutionMetadata = new TaskExecutionMetadata(builders.keySet(), pausedTopologies, processingMode);\n    }\n\n    // Need to (re)set the log here to pick up the `processId` part of the clientId in the prefix\n    public void setLog(final LogContext logContext) {\n        log = logContext.logger(getClass());\n    }\n    \n    public ProcessingMode processingMode() {\n        return processingMode;\n    }\n\n    public long topologyVersion() {\n        return version.topologyVersion.get();\n    }\n\n    private void lock() {\n        version.topologyLock.lock();\n    }\n\n    private void unlock() {\n        version.topologyLock.unlock();\n    }\n\n    public Collection<String> sourceTopicsForTopology(final String name) {\n        return builders.get(name).fullSourceTopicNames();\n    }\n\n    public boolean needsUpdate(final String threadName) {\n        return threadVersions.get(threadName) < topologyVersion();\n    }\n\n    public void registerThread(final String threadName) {\n        threadVersions.put(threadName, 0L);\n    }\n\n    public void unregisterThread(final String threadName) {\n        threadVersions.remove(threadName);\n        maybeNotifyTopologyVersionListeners();\n    }\n\n    public TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata() {\n        return taskExecutionMetadata;\n    }\n\n    public void executeTopologyUpdatesAndBumpThreadVersion(final Consumer<Set<String>> handleTopologyAdditions,\n                                                           final Consumer<Set<String>> handleTopologyRemovals) {\n        try {\n            version.topologyLock.lock();\n            final long latestTopologyVersion = topologyVersion();\n            handleTopologyAdditions.accept(namedTopologiesView());\n            handleTopologyRemovals.accept(namedTopologiesView());\n            threadVersions.put(Thread.currentThread().getName(), latestTopologyVersion);\n        } finally {\n            version.topologyLock.unlock();\n        }\n    }\n\n    public void maybeNotifyTopologyVersionListeners() {\n        try {\n            lock();\n            final long minThreadVersion = minimumThreadVersion();\n            final Iterator<TopologyVersionListener> iterator = version.activeTopologyUpdateListeners.listIterator();\n            TopologyVersionListener topologyVersionListener;\n            while (iterator.hasNext()) {\n                topologyVersionListener = iterator.next();\n                final long topologyVersionWaitersVersion = topologyVersionListener.topologyVersion;\n                if (minThreadVersion >= topologyVersionWaitersVersion) {\n                    topologyVersionListener.future.complete(null);\n                    iterator.remove();\n                    log.info(\"All threads are now on topology version {}\", topologyVersionListener.topologyVersion);\n                }\n            }\n        } finally {\n            unlock();\n        }\n    }\n\n    // Return the minimum version across all live threads, or Long.MAX_VALUE if there are no threads running\n    private long minimumThreadVersion() {\n        final Optional<Long> minVersion = threadVersions.values().stream().min(Long::compare);\n        return minVersion.orElse(Long.MAX_VALUE);\n    }\n\n    public void wakeupThreads() {\n        try {\n            lock();\n            version.topologyCV.signalAll();\n        } finally {\n            unlock();\n        }\n    }\n\n    public void registerLifecycleObserver(final TopologyLifecycleObserver observer) {\n        lock();\n        try {\n            lifecycleObservers.add(observer);\n        } finally {\n            unlock();\n        }\n    }\n\n    private void notifyLifecycleObservers() {\n        final long currentVersion = version.topologyVersion.get();\n        final Set<String> activeTopologies = namedTopologiesView();\n        for (final TopologyLifecycleObserver observer : lifecycleObservers) {\n            try {\n                observer.onTopologyChange(currentVersion, activeTopologies);\n            } catch (final Exception e) {\n                log.warn(\"Topology lifecycle observer threw exception\", e);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    public boolean isTaskTopologyStale(final TaskId taskId) {\n        lock();\n        try {\n            final String topologyName = taskId.topologyName();\n            if (topologyName != null) {\n                return !builders.containsKey(topologyName);\n            }\n            return false;\n        } finally {\n            unlock();\n        }\n    }\n\n    public void maybeWaitForNonEmptyTopology(final Supplier<StreamThread.State> threadState) {\n        if (isEmpty() && threadState.get().isAlive()) {\n            try {\n                lock();\n                while (isEmpty() && threadState.get().isAlive()) {\n                    try {\n                        log.debug(\"Detected that the topology is currently empty, waiting for something to process\");\n                        version.topologyCV.await();\n                    } catch (final InterruptedException e) {\n                        log.error(\"StreamThread was interrupted while waiting on empty topology\", e);\n                    }\n                }\n            } finally {\n                unlock();\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Adds the topology and registers a future that listens for all threads on the older version to see the update\n     */\n    public void registerAndBuildNewTopology(final KafkaFutureImpl<Void> future, final InternalTopologyBuilder newTopologyBuilder) {\n        try {\n            lock();\n            buildAndVerifyTopology(newTopologyBuilder);\n            log.info(\"New NamedTopology {} passed validation and will be added, old topology version is {}\", newTopologyBuilder.topologyName(), version.topologyVersion.get());\n            version.topologyVersion.incrementAndGet();\n            version.activeTopologyUpdateListeners.add(new TopologyVersionListener(topologyVersion(), future));\n            builders.put(newTopologyBuilder.topologyName(), newTopologyBuilder);\n            wakeupThreads();\n            notifyLifecycleObservers();\n            log.info(\"Added NamedTopology {} and updated topology version to {}\", newTopologyBuilder.topologyName(), version.topologyVersion.get());\n        } catch (final Throwable throwable) {\n            log.error(\"Failed to add NamedTopology {}, please retry the operation.\", newTopologyBuilder.topologyName());\n            future.completeExceptionally(throwable);\n        } finally {\n            unlock();\n        }\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Pauses a topology by name\n     * @param topologyName Name of the topology to pause\n     */\n    public void pauseTopology(final String topologyName) {\n        pausedTopologies.add(topologyName);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Checks if a given topology is paused.\n     * @param topologyName If null, assume that we are checking the `UNNAMED_TOPOLOGY`.\n     * @return A boolean indicating if the topology is paused.\n     */\n    public boolean isPaused(final String topologyName) {\n        return pausedTopologies.contains(getTopologyNameOrElseUnnamed(topologyName));\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Resumes a topology by name\n     * @param topologyName Name of the topology to resume\n     */\n    public void resumeTopology(final String topologyName) {\n        pausedTopologies.remove(topologyName);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * Removes the topology and registers a future that listens for all threads on the older version to see the update\n     */\n    public KafkaFuture<Void> unregisterTopology(final KafkaFutureImpl<Void> removeTopologyFuture,\n                                                final String topologyName) {\n        try {\n            lock();\n            log.info(\"Beginning removal of NamedTopology {}, old topology version is {}\", topologyName, version.topologyVersion.get());\n            version.topologyVersion.incrementAndGet();\n            version.activeTopologyUpdateListeners.add(new TopologyVersionListener(topologyVersion(), removeTopologyFuture));\n            final InternalTopologyBuilder removedBuilder = builders.remove(topologyName);\n            removedBuilder.fullSourceTopicNames().forEach(allInputTopics::remove);\n            removedBuilder.allSourcePatternStrings().forEach(allInputTopics::remove);\n            notifyLifecycleObservers();\n            log.info(\"Finished removing NamedTopology {}, topology version was updated to {}\", topologyName, version.topologyVersion.get());\n        } catch (final Throwable throwable) {\n            log.error(\"Failed to remove NamedTopology {}, please retry.\", topologyName);\n            removeTopologyFuture.completeExceptionally(throwable);\n        } finally {\n            unlock();\n        }\n        return removeTopologyFuture;\n    }\n\n    public TaskConfig taskConfig(final TaskId taskId) {\n        final InternalTopologyBuilder builder = lookupBuilderForTask(taskId);\n        return builder.topologyConfigs().getTaskConfig();\n    }\n\n    public void buildAndRewriteTopology() {\n        applyToEachBuilder(this::buildAndVerifyTopology);\n    }\n\n    private void buildAndVerifyTopology(final InternalTopologyBuilder builder) {\n        builder.rewriteTopology(config);\n        builder.buildTopology();\n\n        final Set<String> allInputTopicsCopy = new HashSet<>(allInputTopics);\n\n        // As we go, check each topology for overlap in the set of input topics/patterns\n        final int numInputTopics = allInputTopicsCopy.size();\n        final List<String> inputTopics = builder.fullSourceTopicNames();\n        final Collection<String> inputPatterns = builder.allSourcePatternStrings();\n\n        final Set<String> newInputTopics = new HashSet<>(inputTopics);\n        newInputTopics.addAll(inputPatterns);\n\n        final int numNewInputTopics = newInputTopics.size();\n        allInputTopicsCopy.addAll(newInputTopics);\n\n        if (allInputTopicsCopy.size() != numInputTopics + numNewInputTopics) {\n            inputTopics.retainAll(allInputTopicsCopy);\n            inputPatterns.retainAll(allInputTopicsCopy);\n            log.error(\"Tried to add the NamedTopology {} but it had overlap with other input topics {} or patterns {}\",\n                      builder.topologyName(), inputTopics, inputPatterns);\n            throw new TopologyException(\"Named Topologies may not subscribe to the same input topics or patterns\");\n        }\n\n        final ProcessorTopology globalTopology = builder.buildGlobalStateTopology();\n        if (globalTopology != null) {\n            if (builder.topologyName() != null) {\n                throw new IllegalStateException(\"Global state stores are not supported with Named Topologies\");\n            } else if (this.globalTopology != null) {\n                throw new TopologyException(\"Topology builder had global state, but global topology has already been set\");\n            } else {\n                this.globalTopology = globalTopology;\n                globalStateStores.putAll(builder.globalStateStores());\n            }\n        }\n        allInputTopics.addAll(newInputTopics);\n    }\n\n    public int numStreamThreads(final StreamsConfig config) {\n        final int configuredNumStreamThreads = config.getInt(StreamsConfig.NUM_STREAM_THREADS_CONFIG);\n\n        // If there are named topologies but some are empty, this indicates a bug in user code\n        if (hasNamedTopologies()) {\n            if (hasNoLocalTopology()) {\n                log.error(\"Detected a named topology with no input topics, a named topology may not be empty.\");\n                throw new TopologyException(\"Topology has no stream threads and no global threads, \" +\n                                                \"must subscribe to at least one source topic or pattern.\");\n            }\n        } else {\n            // If both the global and non-global topologies are empty, this indicates a bug in user code\n            if (hasNoLocalTopology() && !hasGlobalTopology()) {\n                log.error(\"Topology with no input topics will create no stream threads and no global thread.\");\n                throw new TopologyException(\"Topology has no stream threads and no global threads, \" +\n                                                \"must subscribe to at least one source topic or global table.\");\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Lastly we check for an empty non-global topology and override the threads to zero if set otherwise\n        if (configuredNumStreamThreads != 0 && hasNoLocalTopology()) {\n            log.info(\"Overriding number of StreamThreads to zero for global-only topology\");\n            return 0;\n        }\n\n        return configuredNumStreamThreads;\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @return true iff the app is using named topologies, or was started up with no topology at all\n     */\n    public boolean hasNamedTopologies() {\n        return !builders.containsKey(UNNAMED_TOPOLOGY);\n    }\n\n    public Set<String> namedTopologiesView() {\n        return hasNamedTopologies() ? Collections.unmodifiableSet(builders.keySet()) : emptySet();\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @return true iff any of the topologies have a global topology\n     */\n    public boolean hasGlobalTopology() {\n        return evaluateConditionIsTrueForAnyBuilders(InternalTopologyBuilder::hasGlobalStores);\n    }\n\n    /**\n     * @return true iff any of the topologies have no local (aka non-global) topology\n     */\n    public boolean hasNoLocalTopology() {\n        return evaluateConditionIsTrueForAnyBuilders(InternalTopologyBuilder::hasNoLocalTopology);\n    }\n\n    public boolean hasPersistentStores() {\n        // If the app is using named topologies, there may not be any persistent state when it first starts up\n        // but a new NamedTopology may introduce it later, so we must return true\n        if (hasNamedTopologies()) {\n            return true;\n        }\n        return evaluateConditionIsTrueForAnyBuilders(InternalTopologyBuilder::hasPersistentStores);\n    }\n\n    public boolean hasStore(final String name) {\n        return evaluateConditionIsTrueForAnyBuilders(b -> b.hasStore(name));\n    }\n\n    public boolean hasOffsetResetOverrides() {\n        // Return true if using named topologies, as there may be named topologies added later which do have overrides\n        return hasNamedTopologies() || evaluateConditionIsTrueForAnyBuilders(InternalTopologyBuilder::hasOffsetResetOverrides);\n    }\n\n    public Optional<AutoOffsetResetStrategy> offsetResetStrategy(final String topic) {\n        for (final InternalTopologyBuilder builder : builders.values()) {\n            if (builder.containsTopic(topic)) {\n                return Optional.ofNullable(builder.offsetResetStrategy(topic));\n            }\n        }\n        log.warn(\"Unable to look up offset reset strategy for topic {} \" +\n            \"as this topic does not appear in the sources of any of the current topologies: {}\\n \" +\n                \"This may be due to natural race condition when removing a topology but it should not \" +\n                \"persist or appear frequently.\",\n            topic, namedTopologiesView()\n        );\n        // returning `null` for an Optional return type triggers spotbugs\n        // we added an exception for NP_OPTIONAL_RETURN_NULL for this method\n        // when we remove NamedTopologies, we can remove this exception\n        return null;\n    }\n\n\n    public Collection<String> fullSourceTopicNamesForTopology(final String topologyName) {\n        Objects.requireNonNull(topologyName, \"topology name must not be null\");\n        return lookupBuilderForNamedTopology(topologyName).fullSourceTopicNames();\n    }\n\n    public Collection<String> allFullSourceTopicNames() {\n        final List<String> sourceTopics = new ArrayList<>();\n        applyToEachBuilder(b -> sourceTopics.addAll(b.fullSourceTopicNames()));\n        return sourceTopics;\n    }\n\n    Pattern sourceTopicPattern() {\n        final StringBuilder patternBuilder = new StringBuilder();\n\n        applyToEachBuilder(b -> {\n            final String patternString = b.sourceTopicPatternString();\n            if (!patternString.isEmpty()) {\n                patternBuilder.append(patternString).append(\"|\");\n            }\n        });\n\n        if (patternBuilder.length() > 0) {\n            patternBuilder.setLength(patternBuilder.length() - 1);\n            return Pattern.compile(patternBuilder.toString());\n        } else {\n            return EMPTY_ZERO_LENGTH_PATTERN;\n        }\n    }\n\n    public boolean usesPatternSubscription() {\n        return evaluateConditionIsTrueForAnyBuilders(InternalTopologyBuilder::usesPatternSubscription);\n    }\n\n    // Can be empty if app is started up with no Named Topologies, in order to add them on later\n    public boolean isEmpty() {\n        return builders.isEmpty();\n    }\n\n    public String topologyDescriptionString() {\n        if (isEmpty()) {\n            return \"\";\n        }\n        final {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01GWjgVH8is9w7Dz8apqXcXa","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"thinking","thinking":"Now I have a good understanding of the codebase. Let me identify the bugs:\n\n**Bug 1: Deadlock between TopologyMetadata.topologyLock and DefaultTaskManager.tasksLock**\n\nPath A (topologyLock → tasksLock):\n- `TopologyMetadata.registerAndBuildNewTopology()` or `unregisterTopology()` acquires `topologyLock` \n- Calls `notifyLifecycleObservers()` which calls `TaskTopologyObserver.onTopologyChange()`\n- Which calls `validateTopologyConsistency()` → `executeWithTasksLocked()` which acquires `tasksLock`\n\n\n- Meanwhile, Path B creates the reverse lock ordering: `DefaultTaskManager.assignNextTask()` grabs `tasksLock`, then calls `topologyMetadata.isTaskTopologyStale()` which tries to acquire `topologyLock`. This creates an ABBA deadlock cycle.\n\n**Fix for Bug 1:**\nThe simplest solution is to remove the lock from `isTaskTopologyStale()` since the underlying `builders` map is a `ConcurrentSkipListMap` where `containsKey()` is already thread-safe, breaking the cycle without altering the semantics.\n\n**Bug 2: Potential hang in DefaultStateUpdater - lost wakeup**\n\nNow I'm looking at the `waitIfAllChangelogsCompletelyRead()` method, which uses a condition variable to wait when all work is done. The issue is that `isIdle` gets set to true before awaiting, but then immediately set to false in the finally block when the thread wakes up, creating a race condition where a signal might be lost if another thread tries to wake this one up.\n\nThe real problem emerges in the `shutdown()` sequence: it sets `isRunning` to false first, then signals the condition variable. But if the waiting thread checks `allWorkDone()` between these two steps, it sees `isRunning` is false and exits the loop without actually being signaled, meaning a subsequent signal gets ignored because the thread is no longer waiting. false and exits the wait loop. That's correct. But there's a potential race condition: between checking `allWorkDone()` returns true and calling `await()`, another thread could add a task and signal the condition. However, since both the check and the signal happen while holding `tasksAndActionsLock`, the signal will wait until the thread is actually in the await state. Now I'm looking at `allWorkDone()` checking `updatingTasks.isEmpty()` on the `StateUpdaterThread` - when `performActionsOnTasks()` acquires the lock, processes the queue, and releases it, there's a subsequent call that I need to trace through.\n\nActually, `updatingTasks` is only modified by the `StateUpdaterThread` itself, so it's single-threaded for writes, and the reads inside `allWorkDone()` are safe. The `tasksAndActions.isEmpty()` check happens under the lock. But I'm noticing that in `waitIfAllChangelogsCompletelyRead()`, the `changelogReader.allChangelogsCompleted()` check isn't protected by the lock.\n\nLet me focus on the actual reproducible bugs. The clear deadlock is between `topologyLock` and `tasksLock`: one thread acquires `topologyLock` in `registerAndBuildNewTopology` and then tries to acquire `tasksLock`, while another thread holds `tasksLock` and tries to acquire `topologyLock`.\n\nFor the second bug, I'm examining the `wakeupThreads()` call in `registerAndBuildNewTopology()` more carefully...\n\nActually, looking at how `wakeupThreads()` re-acquires the `topologyLock` and signals the condition variable while holding it, that's fine. But now I need to trace through `maybeWaitForNonEmptyTopology()` to see if there's a waiting issue there.\n\nThe problem is that the initial `isEmpty()` check happens without holding the lock, so between that check and acquiring the lock, a topology could be added and the signal could fire. Then we'd enter the while loop and call `await()` on a signal that already happened, causing us to wait indefinitely. Though the while loop does re-check `isEmpty()` while holding the lock, so if a topology was added, we'd exit immediately rather than waiting.\n\nActually, thinking through the race condition more carefully: if the thread sees the topology is empty without the lock, then another thread adds a topology and signals before we acquire the lock, we'll still be fine because once we get the lock and check `isEmpty()` again in the while condition, it'll return false and we won't enter the loop. The outer check is just an optimization to avoid the lock overhead when unnecessary—the real safety comes from the guarded check inside the loop.\n\nNow I need to look at the `DefaultTaskManager` to find the second bug.\n\nExamining the task executor's await logic...","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2227,"cache_read_input_tokens":62630,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":2227,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bea6d363-fa8a-4106-81c5-e4c086a94109","uuid":"98f76c57-e4ff-4df1-b5db-7bf6ab7cc5cb"}

             taskReleaseFuture.complete(null);\n    94→                }\n    95→            }\n    96→        }\n    97→\n    98→        private void handleException(final StreamsException e) {\n    99→            if (currentTask != null) {\n   100→                taskManager.setUncaughtException(e, currentTask.id());\n   101→\n   102→                log.debug(\"Releasing task {} due to uncaught exception.\", currentTask.id());\n   103→                unassignCurrentTask();\n   104→            } else {\n   105→                // If we do not currently have a task assigned and still get an error, this is fatal for the executor thread\n   106→                throw e;\n   107→            }\n   108→        }\n   109→\n   110→        private void runOnce(final long nowMs) {\n   111→            handleTaskReleaseRequested();\n   112→\n   113→            if (currentTask == null) {\n   114→                currentTask = taskManager.assignNextTask(DefaultTaskExecutor.this);\n   115→            }\n   116→\n   117→            if (currentTask == null) {\n   118→                try {\n   119→                    taskManager.awaitProcessableTasks(shutdownRequested::get);\n   120→                } } else {\n   124→                boolean progressed = false;\n   125→\n   126→                if (taskExecutionMetadata.canProcessTask(currentTask, nowMs) && currentTask.isProcessable(nowMs)) {\n   127→                    if (processTask(currentTask, nowMs, time)) {\n   128→                        log.trace(\"processed a record for {}\", currentTask.id());\n   129→                        progressed = true;\n   130→                    }\n   131→                }\n   132→\n   133→                if (taskExecutionMetadata.canPunctuateTask(currentTask)) {\n   134→                    if (currentTask.maybePunctuateStreamTime()) {\n   135→                        log.trace(\"punctuated stream time for task {} \", currentTask.id());\n   136→                        progressed = true;\n   137→                    }\n   138→                    if (currentTask.maybePunctuateSystemTime()) {\n   139→                        log.trace(\"punctuated system time for task {} \", currentTask.id());\n   140→                        progressed = true;\n   141→                    }\n   142→                }\n   143→\n   144→                if (!progressed) {\n   145→                    log.debug(\"Releasing task {} because we are not making progress.\", currentTask.id());\n   146→                    unassignCurrentTask();\n   147→                }\n   148→            }\n   149→        }\n   150→\n   151→        private boolean processTask(final Task task, final long now, final Time time) {\n   152→            boolean processed = false;\n   153→            try {\n   154→                processed = task.process(now);\n   155→                if (processed) {\n   156→                    log.trace(\"Successfully processed task {}\", task.id());\n   157→                    task.clearTaskTimeout();\n   158→                    // TODO: enable regardless of whether using named topologies\n   159→                    if (taskExecutionMetadata.hasNamedTopologies() && taskExecutionMetadata.processingMode() != EXACTLY_ONCE_V2) {\n   160→                        taskExecutionMetadata.addToSuccessfullyProcessed(task);\n   161→                    }\n   162→                }\n   163→            } catch (final TimeoutException timeoutException) {\n   164→                // TODO consolidate TimeoutException retries with general error handling\n   165→                task.maybeInitTaskTimeoutOrThrow(now, timeoutException);\n   166→                log.error(\n   167→                    String.format(\n   168→                        \"Could not complete processing records for %s due to the following exception; will move to next task and retry later\",\n   169→                        task.id()),\n   170→                    timeoutException\n   171→                );\n   172→            } catch (final TaskMigratedException e) {\n   173→                log.info(\"Failed to process stream task {} since it got migrated to another thread already. \" +\n   174→                    \"Will trigger a new rebalance and close all tasks as zombies together.\", task.id());\n   175→                throw e;\n   176→            } catch (final StreamsException e) {\n   177→                log.error(String.format(\"Failed to process stream task %s due to the following error:\", task.id()), e);\n   178→                e.setTaskId(task.id());\n   179→                throw              throw new StreamsException(e, task.id());\n   183→            } finally {\n   184→                task.recordProcessBatchTime(time.milliseconds() - now);\n   185→            }\n   186→            return processed;\n   187→        }\n   188→\n   189→        private StreamTask unassignCurrentTask() {\n   190→            if (currentTask == null)\n   191→                throw new IllegalStateException(\"Does not own any task while being ask to unassign from task manager\");\n   192→\n   193→            // flush the task before giving it back to task manager, if we are not handing it back because of an error.\n   194→            if (!taskManager.hasUncaughtException(currentTask.id())) {\n   195→                try {\n   196→                    currentTask.flush();\n   197→                } catch (final StreamsException e) {\n   198→                    log.error(String.format(\"Failed to flush stream task %s due to the following error:\", currentTask.id()), e);\n   199→                    e.setTaskId(currentTask.id());\n   200→                    taskManager.setUncaughtException(e, currentTask.id());\n   201→                } catch (final RuntimeException e) {\n   202→                    log.error(String.format(\"Failed to flush stream task %s due to the following error:\", currentTask.id()), e);\n   203→                    taskManager.setUncaughtException(new StreamsException(e, currentTask.id()), currentTask.id());\n   204→                }\n   205→            }\n   206→            taskManager.unassignTask(currentTask, DefaultTaskExecutor.this);\n   207→\n   208→            final StreamTask retTask = currentTask;\n   209→            currentTask = null;\n   210→            return retTask;\n   211→        }\n   212→    }\n   213→\n   214→    private final Time time;\n   215→    private final String name;\n   216→    private final TaskManager taskManager;\n   217→    private final TaskExecutionMetadata 223→    public DefaultTaskExecutor(final TaskManager taskManager,\n   224→                               final String name,\n   225→                               final Time time,\n   226→ 233→    }\n   234→\n   235→    @Override\n   236→    public String name() {\n   237→        return name;\n   238→    }\n   239→\n   240→    @Override\n   241→    public void start() {\n   242→ 248→    @Override\n   249→    public boolean isRunning() {\n   250→        return taskExecutorThread != null && taskExecutorThread.isAlive();\n   251→    }\n   252→\n   253→    @Override\n   254→ * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more\n * contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file distributed with\n * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.\n * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0\n * (the \"License\"); you may not use this file except in compliance with\n * the License. limitations under the License.\n */\npackage org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.internals.tasks;\n\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.KafkaFuture;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.errors.TimeoutException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.internals.KafkaFutureImpl;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.LogContext;\nimport org.apache.kafka.common.utils.Time;\nimport org.apache.kafka.streams.errors.StreamsException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.streams.errors.TaskMigratedException;\nimport org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.internals.ProcessingThread;\nimport org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.internals.ReadOnlyTask;\nimport org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.internals.StreamTask;\nimport org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.internals.Task;\nimport org.apache.kafka.streams.processor.internals.TaskExecutionMetadata;\n\nimport org.slf4j.Logger;\n\nimport java.time.Duration;\nimport java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicBoolean;\nimport java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicReference;\n\nimport static org.apache.kafka.streams.internals.StreamsConfigUtils.ProcessingMode.EXACTLY_ONCE_V2;\n\npublic class DefaultTaskExecutor implements TaskExecutor {\n\n    private class TaskExecutorThread extends Thread implements ProcessingThread {\n\n        private final AtomicBoolean shutdownRequested = new AtomicBoolean(false);\n        private final AtomicReference<KafkaFutureImpl<StreamTask>> taskReleaseRequested = new AtomicReference<>(null);\n\n        private final Logger log;\n\n        public TaskExecutorThread(final String name) {\n            super(name);\n            final String logPrefix = String.format(\"%s \", name);\n            final LogContext logContext = new LogContext(logPrefix);\n            log = logContext.logger(DefaultTaskExecutor.class);\n        }\n\n        @Override\n        public void run() {\n            log.info(\"Task executor thread started\");\n            try {\n                while (!shutdownRequested.get()) {\n                    try {\n                        runOnce(time.milliseconds());\n                    } catch (final StreamsException e) {\n                        handleException(e);\n                    } catch (final Exception e) {\n                        handleException(new StreamsException(e));\n                    }\n                }\n            } finally {\n                if (currentTask != null) {\n                    log.debug(\"Releasing task {} due to shutdown.\", currentTask.id());\n                    unassignCurrentTask();\n                }\n\n                final KafkaFutureImpl<StreamTask> taskReleaseFuture;\n                if ((taskReleaseFuture = taskReleaseRequested.getAndSet(null)) != null) {\n                    log.debug(\"Asked to return current task, but shutting down.\");\n                    taskReleaseFuture.complete(null);\n                }\n                log.info(\"Task executor thread shutdown\");\n            }\n        }\n\n        private void handleTaskReleaseRequested() {\n            final KafkaFutureImpl<StreamTask> taskReleaseFuture;\n            if ((taskReleaseFuture = taskReleaseRequested.getAndSet(null)) != null) {\n                if (currentTask != null) {\n                    log.debug(\"Releasing task {} upon request.\", currentTask.id());\n                    final StreamTask unassignedTask = unassignCurrentTask();\n                    taskReleaseFuture.complete(unassignedTask);\n                } else {\n                    log.debug(\"Asked to return current task, but returned current task already.\");\n                    taskReleaseFuture.complete(null);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        private void handleException(final StreamsException e) {\n            if (currentTask != null) {\n                taskManager.setUncaughtException(e, currentTask.id());\n\n                log.debug(\"Releasing task {} due to uncaught exception.\", currentTask.id());\n                unassignCurrentTask();\n            } else {\n                // If we do not currently have a task assigned and still get an error, this is fatal for the executor thread\n                throw e;\n            }\n        }\n\n        private void runOnce(final long nowMs) {\n            handleTaskReleaseRequested();\n\n            if (currentTask == null) {\n                currentTask = taskManager.assignNextTask(DefaultTaskExecutor.this);\n            }\n\n            if (currentTask == null) {\n                try {\n                    taskManager.awaitProcessableTasks(shutdownRequested::get);\n                } catch (final InterruptedException ignored) {\n                    // Can be ignored, the cause of the interrupted will be handled in the event loop\n                }\n            } else {\n                boolean progressed = false;\n\n                if (taskExecutionMetadata.canProcessTask(currentTask, nowMs) && currentTask.isProcessable(nowMs)) {\n                    if (processTask(currentTask, nowMs, time)) {\n                        log.trace(\"processed a record for {}\", currentTask.id());\n                        progressed = true;\n                    }\n                }\n\n                if (taskExecutionMetadata.canPunctuateTask(currentTask)) {\n                    if (currentTask.maybePunctuateStreamTime()) {\n                        log.trace(\"punctuated stream time for task {} \", currentTask.id());\n                        progressed = true;\n                    }\n                    if (currentTask.maybePunctuateSystemTime()) {\n                        log.trace(\"punctuated system time for task {} \", currentTask.id());\n                        progressed = true;\n                    }\n                }\n\n                if (!progressed) {\n                    log.debug(\"Releasing task {} because we are not making progress.\", currentTask.id());\n                    unassignCurrentTask();\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        private boolean processTask(final Task task, final long now, final Time time) {\n            boolean processed = false;\n            try {\n                processed = task.process(now);\n                if (processed) {\n                    log.trace(\"Successfully processed task {}\", task.id());\n                    task.clearTaskTimeout();\n                    // TODO: enable regardless of whether using named topologies\n                    if (taskExecutionMetadata.hasNamedTopologies() && taskExecutionMetadata.processingMode() != EXACTLY_ONCE_V2) {\n                        taskExecutionMetadata.addToSuccessfullyProcessed(task);\n                    }\n                }\n            } catch (final TimeoutException timeoutException) {\n                // TODO consolidate TimeoutException retries with general error handling\n                task.maybeInitTaskTimeoutOrThrow(now, timeoutException);\n                log.error(\n                    String.format(\n                        \"Could not complete processing records for %s due to the following exception; will move to next task and retry later\",\n                        task.id()),\n                    timeoutException\n                );\n            } catch (final TaskMigratedException e) {\n                log.info(\"Failed to process stream task {} since it got migrated to another thread already. \" +\n                    \"Will trigger a new rebalance and close all tasks as zombies together.\", task.id());\n                throw e;\n            } catch (final StreamsException e) {\n                log.error(String.format(\"Failed to process stream task %s due to the following error:\", task.id()), e);\n                e.setTaskId(task.id());\n                throw e;\n            } catch (final RuntimeException e) {\n                log.error(String.format(\"Failed to process stream task %s due to the following error:\", task.id()), e);\n                throw new StreamsException(e, task.id());\n            } finally {\n                task.recordProcessBatchTime(time.milliseconds() - now);\n            }\n            return processed;\n        }\n\n        private StreamTask unassignCurrentTask() {\n            if (currentTask == null)\n                throw new IllegalStateException(\"Does not own any task while being ask to unassign from task manager\");\n\n            // flush the task before giving it back to task manager, if we are not handing it back because of an error.\n            if (!taskManager.hasUncaughtException(currentTask.id())) {\n                try {\n                    currentTask.flush();\n                } catch (final StreamsException e) {\n                    log.error(String.format(\"Failed to flush stream task %s due to the following error:\", currentTask.id()), e);\n                    e.setTaskId(currentTask.id());\n                    taskManager.setUncaughtException(e, currentTask.id());\n                } catch (final RuntimeException e) {\n                    log.error(String.format(\"Failed to flush stream task %s due to the following error:\", currentTask.id()), e);\n                    taskManager.setUncaughtException(new StreamsException(e, currentTask.id()), currentTask.id());\n                }\n            }\n            taskManager.unassignTask(currentTask, DefaultTaskExecutor.this);\n\n            final StreamTask retTask = currentTask;\n            currentTask = null;\n            return retTask;\n        }\n    }\n\n    private final Time time;\n    private final String name;\n    private final TaskManager taskManager;\n    private final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata;\n    private final Logger log;\n\n    private StreamTask currentTask = null;\n    private TaskExecutorThread taskExecutorThread = null;\n\n    public DefaultTaskExecutor(final TaskManager taskManager,\n                               final String name,\n                               final Time time,\n                               final TaskExecutionMetadata taskExecutionMetadata) {\n        this.time = time;\n        this.name = name;\n        this.taskManager = taskManager;\n        this.taskExecutionMetadata = taskExecutionMetadata;\n        final LogContext logContext = new LogContext(name);\n        this.log = logContext.logger(DefaultTaskExecutor.class);\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public String name() {\n        return name;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void start() {\n        if (taskExecutorThread == null) {\n            taskExecutorThread = new TaskExecutorThread(name);\n            taskExecutorThread.start();\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public boolean isRunning() {\n        return taskExecutorThread != null && taskExecutorThread.isAlive();\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void requestShutdown() {\n        if (taskExecutorThread != null) {\n            taskExecutorThread.shutdownRequested.set(true);\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public void awaitShutdown(final Duration timeout) {\n        if (taskExecutorThread != null) {\n            try {\n                taskExecutorThread.join(timeout.toMillis());\n                if (taskExecutorThread.isAlive()) {\n                    throw new StreamsException(\"State updater thread did not shutdown within the timeout\");\n                }\n                taskExecutorThread = null;\n            } catch (final InterruptedException ignored) {\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public ReadOnlyTask currentTask() {\n        return currentTask != null ? new ReadOnlyTask(currentTask) : null;\n    }\n\n    @Override\n    public KafkaFuture<StreamTask> unassign() {\n        final KafkaFutureImpl<StreamTask> future = new KafkaFutureImpl<>();\n\n        if (taskExecutorThread != null) {\n            log.debug(\"Asking {} to hand back task\", taskExecutorThread.getName());\n            if (!taskExecutorThread.taskReleaseRequested.compareAndSet(null, future)) {\n                throw new IllegalStateException(\"There was already a task release request registered\");\n            }\n            if (!taskExecutorThread.isAlive()) {\n                log.debug(\"Completing future, because task executor was just shut down\");\n                future.complete(null);\n            } else {\n                taskManager.signalTaskExecutors();\n            }\n        } else {\n            log.debug(\"Tried to unassign but no thread is running\");\n            future.complete(null);\n        }\n\n        return future;\n    }\n}\n","numLines":302,"startLine":1,"totalLines":302}}}
